Aang, Swift Savior // Aang and La, Ocean.name: Aang, Swift Savior // Aang and La, Ocean's Fury
Aang, Swift Savior // Aang and La, Ocean.notes: Flash + flying bounce effect is solid tempo. The airbend ability (temporary exile, can be recast for {2}) is a unique disruption tool. The backside transform requires heavy investment ({8} waterbend) and is unlikely to come online in most games, making it mostly irrelevant in Cube. Evaluated primarily as a 3-mana flash flyer with a bounce variant.

Aang's Iceberg.name: Aang's Iceberg
Aang's Iceberg.notes: Flash Oblivion Ring variant with an optional self-sacrifice for scry 2. The scry-2 exit clause adds some value and avoids leaving a permanent for opponents to exploit. Solid in white control but slightly slower and more conditional than top-tier removal.

Abandoned Air Temple.name: Abandoned Air Temple
Abandoned Air Temple.notes: Enters tapped without a basic land, which is a real drawback in multicolor or fetchland-heavy cubes. The +1/+1 counter ability at {3}{W}{T} is very expensive and mostly irrelevant. Functions as a budget Plains in mono-white shells.

Abhorrent Oculus.name: Abhorrent Oculus
Abhorrent Oculus.notes: The additional cost of exiling six graveyard cards is significant but blue decks with self-mill or draw-discard can fuel it. A 3-mana flying threat that manifests dread each opponent's upkeep generates real card advantage over time. Best in blue decks that naturally fill the graveyard.

Abrade.name: Abrade
Abrade.notes: Modal removal is extremely valuable; dealing 3 to a creature or destroying an artifact covers two major threat categories. A staple in any red deck and a sideboard all-star that earns main deck status in Vintage Cube due to the artifact density.

Adeline, Resplendent Cathar.name: Adeline, Resplendent Cathar
Adeline, Resplendent Cathar.notes: Scales power based on creature count and floods the board with tokens on attacks. Exceptionally strong in white aggro and tokens strategies. Vigilance means she attacks without leaving you exposed.

Aether Spellbomb.name: Aether Spellbomb
Aether Spellbomb.notes: Cheap artifact that provides two modes: bounce a creature or draw a card. Both abilities require sacrifice, limiting reuse without recursion. Best in artifact synergy decks that value the cheap artifact for Welder/Tinker chains.

Agatha's Soul Cauldron.name: Agatha's Soul Cauldron
Agatha's Soul Cauldron.notes: Powerful artifact that exiles graveyard cards and grants their activated abilities to your creatures with +1/+1 counters. Combo potential with Devoted Druid + Walking Ballista is real. Also incidentally hates graveyards while building your own engine.

Ajani, Nacatl Pariah // Ajani, Nacatl Av.name: Ajani, Nacatl Pariah // Ajani, Nacatl Avenger
Ajani, Nacatl Pariah // Ajani, Nacatl Av.notes: A 2-mana creature that immediately creates a 2/1 token is already solid value. The planeswalker flip condition (other Cats dying) is easier to achieve in dedicated Cat/aggro builds but even without it, the front side is efficient. The backside offers +1/+1 counter distribution, token creation with conditional damage ping, and a -4 mass removal of non-chosen permanents — all very powerful in RW aggro.

Ancestral Recall.name: Ancestral Recall
Ancestral Recall.notes: One of the most powerful Magic cards ever printed. Drawing three cards for {U} is generically broken and goes in every blue deck. No specific archetype tag needed — it is universally played.

Ancient Tomb.name: Ancient Tomb
Ancient Tomb.notes: Produces two colorless mana for the cost of 2 life. Fits in every archetype that can use colorless mana — which is essentially everything. The life payment is negligible given the tempo advantage it provides.

Anger.name: Anger
Anger.notes: Grants haste to all your creatures as long as it's in the graveyard and you control a Mountain. Synergizes excellently with reanimator strategies that get large threats into play — having them immediately attack is a massive tempo swing. Requires a Mountain in play, which is a real constraint in some builds.

Animate Dead.name: Animate Dead
Animate Dead.notes: One of the core reanimation spells. Also sees play in any black value strategy that self-mills or discards fatties. The -1/-0 is virtually irrelevant on most targets.

Anje's Ravager.name: Anje's Ravager
Anje's Ravager.notes: Forced attack and forced discard is a real cost, but the three-card refill is powerful. Best in aggressive shells that can empty their hand quickly or want to fuel the graveyard. Madness cost makes it cheap to replay off discard effects.

Appa, Steadfast Guardian.name: Appa, Steadfast Guardian
Appa, Steadfast Guardian.notes: Airbending works like a pseudo-flicker/cost-reduction engine. Flash + flying body is relevant for tempo. The token generation from casting spells out of exile rewards building around it but is secondary value.

Archon of Cruelty.name: Archon of Cruelty
Archon of Cruelty.notes: A premier reanimator / cheat target. The ETB+attack trigger swings the game by 6 life and 3 cards plus a sacrifice. Essentially game-ending if it resolves via Sneak Attack or Reanimate.

Arena of Glory.name: Arena of Glory
Arena of Glory.notes: Enters untapped in red decks and can provide haste to creatures as a mana-sink. The exert downside means it skips an untap but the double-red with haste rider is a real upside in aggro.

Arid Mesa.name: Arid Mesa
Arid Mesa.notes: Fetchland that fixes for red and white. Colorless in identity but an S/A-tier mana-fixing staple for any RW-adjacent deck. Powers delirium/threshold and thins the deck.

Arwen, Mortal Queen.name: Arwen, Mortal Queen
Arwen, Mortal Queen.notes: Comes with built-in indestructible and can distribute counters+lifelink. Scales well with counter-synergy shells but is a bit slow and low-impact in faster cubes without dedicated support.

Astrologian's Planisphere.name: Astrologian's Planisphere
Astrologian's Planisphere.notes: Creates its own hero token via Job Select, so it's self-contained. The trigger rewards noncreature-spell-heavy decks. Grows quickly in storm or tempo shells but requires build-around and the token starts small.

Atraxa, Grand Unifier.name: Atraxa, Grand Unifier
Atraxa, Grand Unifier.notes: A top-tier cheat / reanimate target. The ETB draws up to 8 cards across card types, which is often game-winning. The 4-keyword body is also difficult to deal with in combat.

Avacyn's Pilgrim.name: Avacyn's Pilgrim
Avacyn's Pilgrim.notes: One-mana white mana dork. Worse than Noble Hierarch but still provides on-curve acceleration and color fixing for GW strategies. Useful in any green creature-based shell splashing white.

Badgermole Cub.name: Badgermole Cub
Badgermole Cub.notes: The earthbend trigger animates a land as a threat and the mana-tapping creature clause effectively doubles green mana from creatures, which is strong with other mana dorks. Unusual synergy with tap-for-mana creatures.

Badlands.name: Badlands
Badlands.notes: Original dual land. An A-tier mana fixing staple for any BR-adjacent deck. Fetchable and comes into play untapped, making it one of the best lands in the cube.

Balance.name: Balance
Balance.notes: One of the most broken asymmetric board-wipes in the game. Best when you're ahead on board or have ways to empty your own hand. Enables brutal tempo swings in white aggro or prison strategies.

Baleful Mastery.name: Baleful Mastery
Baleful Mastery.notes: The {1}{B} mode giving the opponent a card is a real cost, but exiling any creature or planeswalker at instant speed for 2 mana is extremely efficient. Usually best at full cost when ahead or against key threats.

Baleful Strix.name: Baleful Strix
Baleful Strix.notes: A 2-mana 1/1 flying deathtoucher that replaces itself. Incredible blocker and card-neutral tempo piece. Artifact type opens synergy with Welder and Tinker packages.

Baloth Prime.name: Baloth Prime
Baloth Prime.notes: The stun-counter setup means it takes 6 turns to attack normally without sacrificing lands. Extremely synergistic with land-sacrifice loops (Crucible, Loam, fetchlands), but very narrow outside that context.

Barrowgoyf.name: Barrowgoyf
Barrowgoyf.notes: Scales with graveyard density like Tarmogoyf, but also mills on combat damage, potentially fueling more graveyard payoffs or acting as a soft reanimation engine. Deathtouch and lifelink make it a great combat creature.

Basalt Monolith.name: Basalt Monolith
Basalt Monolith.notes: Produces 3 colorless mana and can combo with Rings of Brighthearth or Power Artifact for infinite colorless mana. Useful fast mana in artifact and storm decks.

Basim Ibn Ishaq.name: Basim Ibn Ishaq
Basim Ibn Ishaq.notes: Unblockable threat that draws cards on historic spells (artifacts, legends, Sagas). Naturally fits artifact-heavy or legend-dense blue-black shells. Grows over time from combat damage.

Basking Rootwalla.name: Basking Rootwalla
Basking Rootwalla.notes: Madness {0} makes this a free threat when discarded, making it an excellent pairing with discard outlets like Faithless Looting or Bazaar of Baghdad. In fair decks it's a weak 1/1 for G, so its value is almost entirely tied to discard synergies.

Bayou.name: Bayou
Bayou.notes: Dual land enabling BG and three-color combinations. Fetchable and untapped, it's a pure mana-fixing staple.

Bazaar of Baghdad.name: Bazaar of Baghdad
Bazaar of Baghdad.notes: One of the most powerful lands in the Cube. Nets no cards on rate but enables absurdly powerful graveyard strategies from turn 1. Seeing this in a pack is a clear signal to move into dredge or reanimator.

Birds of Paradise.name: Birds of Paradise
Birds of Paradise.notes: The gold standard mana dork. Any-color production and flying make it the best 1-drop accelerant in green. Staple in any green deck wanting early ramp.

Bitter Triumph.name: Bitter Triumph
Bitter Triumph.notes: Destroy any creature or planeswalker at instant speed for {1}{B} with a minor drawback (3 life or discard). The discard cost is often a bonus in graveyard decks. Extremely efficient removal.

Black Lotus.name: Black Lotus
Black Lotus.notes: The most powerful mana accelerant in the game. Fits virtually every archetype but is tagged here for the combo/fast-mana shells that exploit it most. It's so universally good it's not a signpost for any single archetype.

Blackcleave Cliffs.name: Blackcleave Cliffs
Blackcleave Cliffs.notes: Fastland that enters untapped in the early game — ideal for aggro and tempo BR/UBR shells. Generic dual-land staple, not a signpost.

Blazemire Verge.name: Blazemire Verge
Blazemire Verge.notes: Conditional BR dual that requires controlling a Swamp or Mountain — effectively always on in a two-color BR or multicolor deck that fetches basics. Slightly worse than Blood Crypt or Blackcleave Cliffs but fills the same role as the 3rd-4th BR source.

Blazing Rootwalla.name: Blazing Rootwalla
Blazing Rootwalla.notes: Madness {0} means it's essentially a free threat whenever you discard it. Primarily a dredge/reanimator support card that turns looting effects into free creature deployment. Also playable as a cheap beater in red aggro.

Bleachbone Verge.name: Bleachbone Verge
Bleachbone Verge.notes: Conditional BW dual requiring a Plains or Swamp in play. Solid mana fixing for WB and Esper shells as a 3rd-4th dual source.

Blightsteel Colossus.name: Blightsteel Colossus
Blightsteel Colossus.notes: The premier Tinker target — a single hit kills on the spot via infect. Seeing this strongly signals tinker-combo is open. Also a payoff in Sneak & Show and Reanimator.

Blood Crypt.name: Blood Crypt
Blood Crypt.notes: Fetchable BR shockland — one of the best mana-fixing duals in the cube. Generic staple for all BR and tri-color shells.

Bloodbraid Challenger.name: Bloodbraid Challenger
Bloodbraid Challenger.notes: Cascade + Haste is a strong value package, and the Escape mode gives late-game resilience. Fits RG and Jund midrange shells that can fill the graveyard. Not quite as impactful as classic Bloodbraid Elf since it doesn't cascade into as many hits at 5 mana.

Bloodchief's Thirst.name: Bloodchief's Thirst
Bloodchief's Thirst.notes: Flexible black removal that handles small threats cheaply and scales up with the kicker. Not quite Fatal Push or Doom Blade in raw efficiency but the modal nature makes it solid in any black midrange or control shell.

Bloodstained Mire.name: Bloodstained Mire
Bloodstained Mire.notes: A fetchland that searches for Swamp or Mountain. Fixes mana, enables shuffle effects, and fuels delirium/threshold. One of the most universally powerful lands in the cube.

Bloodtithe Harvester.name: Bloodtithe Harvester
Bloodtithe Harvester.notes: Creates a Blood token on ETB, providing card selection and discard outlet. The sacrifice ability doubles as removal in a Blood-token-heavy shell. Fits BR aggro and midrange; especially good when you can accumulate Blood tokens.

Blooming Marsh.name: Blooming Marsh
Blooming Marsh.notes: Fastland that enters untapped early — excellent in BG and tri-color shells. Generic dual-land staple.

Bolas's Citadel.name: Bolas's Citadel
Bolas's Citadel.notes: One of the premier Tinker targets in the cube — can chain spells off the top for nearly free and close the game in one turn. Combos particularly well with Sensei's Divining Top (draw-loop) and Aetherflux Reservoir as a redundant storm payoff.

Bone Shards.name: Bone Shards
Bone Shards.notes: The additional cost is a feature in reanimator (discard a fatty) or sacrifice-synergy decks. In aggro it pairs with recursive creatures like Gravecrawler. Unconditional removal at one mana makes it broadly playable.

Bonecrusher Giant // Stomp.name: Bonecrusher Giant // Stomp
Bonecrusher Giant // Stomp.notes: Two-for-one as adventure: Stomp doubles as uncounterable damage-prevention hate (kills in response to Fog/protection), then a solid 4/3 body that punishes targeted removal. Fits almost any red deck.

Boseiju, Who Endures.name: Boseiju, Who Endures
Boseiju, Who Endures.notes: Produces green mana with no downside and doubles as free-roll artifact/enchantment/nonbasic land removal via channel. Exceptional in lands/green decks but splashable anywhere running green.

Botanical Sanctum.name: Botanical Sanctum
Botanical Sanctum.notes: Fastland; enters untapped in the first three turns when it matters most. Solid dual for Simic-based strategies, but unremarkable beyond fixing.

Bountiful Landscape.name: Bountiful Landscape
Bountiful Landscape.notes: Sacrifice-fetch for basic Forest/Island/Mountain is narrow fixing. The cycling cost is expensive at three colored mana. Primarily useful in Temur ramp shells; generally outclassed by fetchlands.

Brain Freeze.name: Brain Freeze
Brain Freeze.notes: Storm kill condition that mills rather than damages. Useful as a secondary storm finisher or in mill-adjacent strategies. Weak outside storm decks since the base effect is tiny.

Brainstorm.name: Brainstorm
Brainstorm.notes: The best cantrip in the format. Universally played in any blue deck; not archetype-specific. Pairs with fetchlands to shuffle away unwanted cards and effectively draw three.

Brazen Borrower // Petty Theft.name: Brazen Borrower // Petty Theft
Brazen Borrower // Petty Theft.notes: Flash flying creature that bounces a problematic permanent first. The adventure provides tempo and the 3/1 flash flier closes games quickly. Premier blue tempo threat.

Breeding Pool.name: Breeding Pool
Breeding Pool.notes: Fetchable dual land; one of the best pieces of fixing in the cube. Enables turn-1 untapped blue or green mana for any Simic or Temur strategy.

Brightglass Gearhulk.name: Brightglass Gearhulk
Brightglass Gearhulk.notes: Tutors up to two 0/1-CMC artifacts, creatures, or enchantments on ETB. Powerful in dedicated low-curve artifact or white weenie shells but too narrow and expensive for general use. Finding two Moxen or key one-drops can be backbreaking.

Bristly Bill, Spine Sower.name: Bristly Bill, Spine Sower
Bristly Bill, Spine Sower.notes: Landfall counter-placement is strong in land-heavy/ramp decks, and the doubling ability can become enormous. Requires significant setup and multiple lands entering to pay off; too slow for aggro, too win-more in non-dedicated builds.

Broadside Bombardiers.name: Broadside Bombardiers
Broadside Bombardiers.notes: The Boast ability scales extremely well with high-MV artifact sacrifices (e.g. Wurmcoil Engine for 8+ damage). In non-artifact decks it still functions as a hasty menace threat with a modest drain stapled on. Best in artifact-synergy or sacrifice-matters shells.

Burst Lightning.name: Burst Lightning
Burst Lightning.notes: A strict downgrade from Lightning Bolt at base, but the kicked mode reaching 4 damage gives it late-game relevance. Competes with Shock/Galvanic Blast in the 1-mana burn slot; solid but not elite.

Cabal Ritual.name: Cabal Ritual
Cabal Ritual.notes: Primarily a storm ritual that can generate explosive mana in the mid-game. Threshold mode is easy to achieve in storm/reanimator decks. Too narrow for generic black decks.

Candelabra of Tawnos.name: Candelabra of Tawnos
Candelabra of Tawnos.notes: An iconic combo piece that turns Tolarian Academy or High Tide into absurd mana engines. Seeing this in a pack is a strong signal to pursue a storm or lands strategy. Also combos with Gaea's Cradle in ramp/lands. Extremely powerful in the right shell but near-useless without synergy targets.

Cankerbloom.name: Cankerbloom
Cankerbloom.notes: A versatile hate piece with an upside proliferate mode. The body is modest but flash-like value on entry plus the proliferate synergy with planeswalkers/counters decks makes it a solid green utility creature. Better than a typical Naturalize because it can proliferate instead.

Carnage Interpreter.name: Carnage Interpreter
Carnage Interpreter.notes: Discarding your hand to investigate four times is a high-risk, high-reward effect. Best in decks that want an empty hand anyway (Lion's Eye Diamond shells, hellbent strategies) or those that can quickly crack Clues. The stat boost to 4/4 menace with few cards in hand is relevant pressure.

Cathar Commando.name: Cathar Commando
Cathar Commando.notes: Flash body plus free sacrifice removal gives this real main-deck playability. Useful for blowing up Aether Vial, Grafdigger's Cage, or opposing hate pieces at instant speed. The 3/1 body is above rate for white weenie.

Caustic Bronco.name: Caustic Bronco
Caustic Bronco.notes: An aggressive 2-drop that provides card advantage at the cost of life when unsaddled. The Saddle 3 requirement is significant but in a creature-dense deck this can turn into a one-sided Dark Confidant on attack. Best in black aggro/midrange decks that can easily saddle it.

Cecil, Dark Knight // Cecil, Redeemed Pa.name: Cecil, Dark Knight // Cecil, Redeemed Paladin
Cecil, Dark Knight // Cecil, Redeemed Pa.notes: A 1-mana deathtouch creature with a built-in transformation condition is intriguing, but the Darkness drawback (losing life equal to damage dealt) and the requirement to reduce to half life to flip make it awkward. Paladin side's indestructible anthem is powerful in a go-wide context, but the hoops are steep. Best in aggressive BW decks that can rapidly flip it.

Celestial Colonnade.name: Celestial Colonnade
Celestial Colonnade.notes: The premier manland for WU decks. A 4/4 flyer/vigilant threat that comes for free in your mana base is extremely difficult for opponents to interact with on sorcery speed. Seeing this strongly signals WU control or tempo is open.

Chain Lightning.name: Chain Lightning
Chain Lightning.notes: Effectively a second Lightning Bolt in most cube contexts. The opponent rarely has {R}{R} up and the copy clause almost never matters. 3 damage at sorcery speed for 1 mana is still format-defining burn.

Chandra, Torch of Defiance.name: Chandra, Torch of Defiance
Chandra, Torch of Defiance.notes: One of the best planeswalkers in the cube. Two different +1 abilities (card advantage/damage and mana generation), a -3 that kills most creatures, and a game-ending ultimate. The mana ability enables explosive turns in any red deck. A true multi-archetype staple.

Channel.name: Channel
Channel.notes: One of the most powerful cards in Cube history — converts life to mana at an absurd rate. Primarily pairs with a large X-spell or fatty finisher. Combo enabler in any green shell that can leverage the mana burst.

Chromatic Star.name: Chromatic Star
Chromatic Star.notes: Replaces itself on death, making it excellent fodder for Welder and other artifact-sacrifice synergies. Cantrip + mana fixing in one package.

Chrome Mox.name: Chrome Mox
Chrome Mox.notes: Fast mana that costs card disadvantage; best in combo decks that need explosive early turns and can afford to exile a card from hand.

City of Traitors.name: City of Traitors
City of Traitors.notes: Produces two colorless mana but sacrifices itself when you play a second land, so it strongly rewards fast-mana or combo builds that exploit the early burst.

Coalition Relic.name: Coalition Relic
Coalition Relic.notes: Three-mana rock that can store a counter for a two-mana burst the following turn. Good in greedy multi-color control or combo lists needing color fixing.

Collective Brutality.name: Collective Brutality
Collective Brutality.notes: Swiss-army-knife black spell. Escalate modes let you discard a fatty for reanimation while simultaneously stripping an opponent's key instant/sorcery and draining life — extremely high ceiling in reanimator and midrange.

Commercial District.name: Commercial District
Commercial District.notes: Surveil 1 on entry offsets the enters-tapped drawback slightly. Useful in RG or Jund shells that can use the self-mill to set up graveyard value.

Concealed Courtyard.name: Concealed Courtyard
Concealed Courtyard.notes: Fastland — effectively untapped on turns 1–3, which is exactly when aggro and tempo decks need it most. Excellent in BW and Esper lists.

Concealing Curtains // Revealing Eye.name: Concealing Curtains // Revealing Eye
Concealing Curtains // Revealing Eye.notes: Cheap one-drop Wall that eventually becomes a menace threat with hand disruption. Acceptable in black disruption strategies but the transform cost is clunky.

Consider.name: Consider
Consider.notes: Instant-speed cantrip with surveil — better than Opt in graveyard-synergy decks. Enables dredge triggers and fuels delve/reanimator setup at minimal cost.

Consult the Star Charts.name: Consult the Star Charts
Consult the Star Charts.notes: Scales with land count, so it can be powerful in the late game but is weak early. The kicked mode is very strong but costs 4 mana total — fairly slow for most blue decks in Cube.

Containment Priest.name: Containment Priest
Containment Priest.notes: Flash body with a hate effect that shuts down Reanimator, Sneak & Show, and Oath of Druids. A two-mana 2/2 flash that also disrupts opponents makes it a staple in white controlling and tempo lists.

Copperline Gorge.name: Copperline Gorge
Copperline Gorge.notes: Fastland that enters untapped on turns 1–3, making it excellent in aggressive and midrange RG shells. Less useful in control or late-game strategies.

Cori-Steel Cutter.name: Cori-Steel Cutter
Cori-Steel Cutter.notes: The Flurry trigger fires on the second spell each turn, making it a natural fit in spell-dense red decks. The token generation plus Equipment attachment is a strong two-for-one engine. Prowess on the Monk synergizes with the storm/spell-count strategies.

Corpse Dance.name: Corpse Dance
Corpse Dance.notes: Buyback makes this a repeatable reanimation engine. Works best with creatures that have enters/dies triggers so the exile clause is a feature not a bug. Pairs extremely well with Goblin Welder loops or any deck stacking the graveyard deliberately.

Council's Judgment.name: Council's Judgment
Council's Judgment.notes: Uniquely sidesteps protection, hexproof, and shroud since it exiles permanents 'with the most votes' rather than targeting. One of white's best answers to problem permanents it can't otherwise touch.

Counterspell.name: Counterspell
Counterspell.notes: The gold standard of hard counters. Goes in every blue deck; not a signpost because it's pure generic blue power.

Courser of Kruphix.name: Courser of Kruphix
Courser of Kruphix.notes: Provides card advantage through land plays, life gain, and a decent 2/4 body. Works especially well with fetchlands to gain life and sculpt the top of the library.

Coveted Jewel.name: Coveted Jewel
Coveted Jewel.notes: The ETB draw-three is powerful but the 'gift it to the attacker' clause is a serious liability in a fast cube environment. Best used as a Tinker target or in stax decks where you can protect it, or in storm where you spend the mana immediately.

Crabomination.name: Crabomination
Crabomination.notes: The Emerge cost requires sacrificing an artifact, making this most at home in artifact-heavy shells where you can reduce its cost significantly. The ETB 'steal' effect is high-variance but can generate enormous value. Fringe playable.

Creeping Tar Pit.name: Creeping Tar Pit
Creeping Tar Pit.notes: One of the best manlands in the format. Enters tapped, but the unblockable threat mode is extremely relevant for closing games in control and tempo shells. UB color fixing is a bonus.

Crop Rotation.name: Crop Rotation
Crop Rotation.notes: At instant speed, fetching Dark Depths + Thespian's Stage for a 20/20 Marit Lage is one of the most powerful plays in the cube. Also fetches Wasteland or Urborg situationally. Strongly signals the Lands archetype.

Crucible of Worlds.name: Crucible of Worlds
Crucible of Worlds.notes: Enables devastating land-lock loops with Wasteland/Strip Mine. Central to the Lands and Stax archetypes. Also incidentally powerful in any fetchland-heavy deck for pure card advantage.

Currency Converter.name: Currency Converter
Currency Converter.notes: Functions as a discard outlet and looting engine at low cost. The exile-from-graveyard clause can interfere with reanimator setups, but the token generation and Treasure mode add flexibility. Niche role-player in discard-synergy decks.

Cut Down.name: Cut Down
Cut Down.notes: Extremely efficient at {B} but the power+toughness restriction means it misses many Cube staples (Tarmogoyf, Wurmcoil Engine, etc.). Best in early-game tempo contexts or decks that need cheap removal.

Dack Fayden.name: Dack Fayden
Dack Fayden.notes: The Vintage Cube's best planeswalker alongside Jace TMS. +1 enables looting for reanimator/storm setups; −2 steals artifacts. Signposts artifacts because artifact theft is so uniquely powerful in that shell.

Damn.name: Damn
Damn.notes: Remarkably flexible: a 2-mana hard removal spell OR a 4-mana Wrath. The BW color identity limits it but the versatility is exceptional for Esper/WB shells.

Damnation.name: Damnation
Damnation.notes: The black Wrath of God. A staple in any black control or midrange deck that needs a sweeper. No regeneration clause is relevant against almost nothing in Cube.

Dark Confidant.name: Dark Confidant
Dark Confidant.notes: Legendary card advantage engine. Life loss is manageable in midrange/control decks with low-to-moderate curves. A defining black two-drop.

Dark Depths.name: Dark Depths
Dark Depths.notes: The primary combo piece of the lands archetype alongside Thespian's Stage or Vampire Hexmage. Creates a 20/20 indestructible flyer. Colorless land that doesn't tap for mana, so it needs enablers.

Dark Ritual.name: Dark Ritual
Dark Ritual.notes: Ritual effects are format-defining mana acceleration in Storm and Reanimator. +2 black mana for {B} fuels explosive early turns. Not a signpost by itself but strongly pulls toward black combo strategies.

Darkslick Shores.name: Darkslick Shores
Darkslick Shores.notes: Solid UB dual that enters untapped early. Slightly worse than a true dual or fetchland but reliable enough for any UB or three-color shell.

Dauthi Voidwalker.name: Dauthi Voidwalker
Dauthi Voidwalker.notes: 2/1 shadow for {BB} is efficient on its own. The graveyard hate is devastating against Reanimator/Dredge, and the activated ability can steal an opponent's best spell for free. Phenomenal hoser that is also just a good card.

Daze.name: Daze
Daze.notes: Free counter in the early game is exceptional in tempo strategies that want to bounce lands anyway. Falls off hard in the late game; best in aggressive blue decks keeping low land counts.

Death-Greeter's Champion.name: Death-Greeter's Champion
Death-Greeter's Champion.notes: Backup 1 grants double strike to another creature, which can be strong in go-wide red aggro or Boros. The Dash mode allows repeated double-strike grants. Niche but functional in the right aggro shell.

Deathrite Shaman.name: Deathrite Shaman
Deathrite Shaman.notes: One of the best one-drops in Cube. Functions as mana acceleration, graveyard hate, and a life drain threat all in one. Thrives in fetch-heavy metas and punishes graveyard strategies. Nearly colorless in practice due to the hybrid cost.

Deep-Cavern Bat.name: Deep-Cavern Bat
Deep-Cavern Bat.notes: Combines disruption and a clock in one body. The exile-until-leaves effect is a strong tempo play reminiscent of Kitesail Freebooter, and lifelink adds incidental life gain relevant in aggressive mirrors.

Delayed Blast Fireball.name: Delayed Blast Fireball
Delayed Blast Fireball.notes: The base mode is underwhelming at 3 mana for 2 damage; real power is in the Foretell upgrade to 5 damage which doubles as a sweeper and a burn spell. Good in token-heavy metas.

Delighted Halfling.name: Delighted Halfling
Delighted Halfling.notes: Primarily valuable when your deck is planeswalker- or legend-heavy. The uncounterability clause is a niche but real upside against blue control decks. Weak outside of legendary-dense builds.

Demonic Tutor.name: Demonic Tutor
Demonic Tutor.notes: One of the most powerful cards in Vintage Cube. Unconditional two-mana tutor is generically busted in every black deck; no single archetype tag needed.

Descendant of Storms.name: Descendant of Storms
Descendant of Storms.notes: A one-drop that can snowball with repeated attacks, but the pump cost of {1}{W} is steep. Better in white weenie than tokens since it needs to attack repeatedly to pay off.

Detective's Phoenix.name: Detective's Phoenix
Detective's Phoenix.notes: The bestow-from-graveyard mechanic is powerful but Collect Evidence 6 is a real cost. Works best in decks that naturally fill the graveyard with high-MV cards. Flying haste is a relevant aggressive threat.

Dismember.name: Dismember
Dismember.notes: Near-universal removal playable in any color at 3-life cost. Especially valuable in decks that want removal but lack black mana sources, or in aggressive red decks that need an answer to large blockers.

Displacer Kitten.name: Displacer Kitten
Displacer Kitten.notes: Blinks your own permanents every time you cast a noncreature spell, generating enormous value with ETB permanents like planeswalkers (re-using loyalty abilities by flickering and replaying). Can enable near-infinite value loops.

Doomsday.name: Doomsday
Doomsday.notes: Combo win condition: stack a 5-card pile and win via Thassa's Oracle or Lab Maniac. Requires build-around and specific support cards; a true signpost that you're going the storm/combo route.

Dragon's Rage Channeler.name: Dragon's Rage Channeler
Dragon's Rage Channeler.notes: One of the best one-drops in the format. Surveil enables delirium quickly and the delirium mode (3/3 flyer that must attack) is devastating. Excellent in any red deck that runs a mix of spell types.

Duress.name: Duress
Duress.notes: One-mana targeted discard hits the cards that matter most. Especially potent in combo decks clearing the path for a kill, and in control as cheap hand disruption.

Eagles of the North.name: Eagles of the North
Eagles of the North.notes: Six mana is very expensive for the Cube, but Plainscycling for {1} makes this basically a cantrip in mono-white/white-heavy decks that want the land. The pump effect on entry is a nice bonus in token-wide strategies.

Earthbender Ascension.name: Earthbender Ascension
Earthbender Ascension.notes: Landfall enchantment that ramps and eventually buffs creatures. 'Earthbend 2' is a non-standard mechanic suggesting land-related abilities. Fits landfall/ramp shells but is slow and conditional; the payoff (a single +1/+1 counter + trample after 4 triggers) is modest for a 3-mana enchantment.

Echo of Eons.name: Echo of Eons
Echo of Eons.notes: Flashback for {2}{U} makes this a potent wheel effect, especially in storm where Lion's Eye Diamond can pay for flashback. Narset locks opponents out while you refill. A near-Timetwister in the right shell.

Elegant Parlor.name: Elegant Parlor
Elegant Parlor.notes: Enters tapped, which is a real cost in a fast format. The surveil 1 on entry provides minor card selection. Playable as a dual land for RW or WU/WUB builds but strictly worse than fetchable dual lands.

Elite Spellbinder.name: Elite Spellbinder
Elite Spellbinder.notes: Flying body with a potent ETB that taxes a key opponent spell by {2}. Combines disruption with a relevant clock; stax-adjacent but broadly useful in white aggro and tempo shells.

Elspeth, Storm Slayer.name: Elspeth, Storm Slayer
Elspeth, Storm Slayer.notes: Token-doubling static ability is extremely powerful in token-dense white decks. The +1 builds a board and the 0 grants flying for alpha strikes. Removal is niche (MV 3+ restriction) but serviceable. Strong in any white aggro or tokens shell.

Elvish Mystic.name: Elvish Mystic
Elvish Mystic.notes: Standard 1-mana green dork; less powerful than Llanowar Elves / Birds of Paradise but fills the same role. Essential in any green ramp or stompy shell.

Elvish Reclaimer.name: Elvish Reclaimer
Elvish Reclaimer.notes: A one-mana tutor for any land on a creature that grows into a threat is a strong signal for the lands archetype. Particularly broken fetching Dark Depths or Wasteland. Gets +2/+2 easily in dedicated lands decks.

Embereth Shieldbreaker // Battle Display.name: Embereth Shieldbreaker // Battle Display
Embereth Shieldbreaker // Battle Display.notes: The adventure half (Battle Display) destroys an artifact at sorcery speed for {R}, and the creature body is a relevant 2/1 for {1}{R}. Flexible and efficient; great answer to fast artifact mana or key combo pieces.

Emperor of Bones.name: Emperor of Bones
Emperor of Bones.notes: Pairs graveyard hate with a reanimation payoff. Adapt triggers the creature-ETB, and the finality counter plus sacrifice clause prevent repeated abuse. Functions as both hate piece and value engine; especially strong in midrange or reanimator shells that can recur large threats.

Emrakul, the Aeons Torn.name: Emrakul, the Aeons Torn
Emrakul, the Aeons Torn.notes: The premier cheat target in the cube. Annihilator 6, an extra turn trigger on cast, and protection from colored spells makes it game-ending the moment it resolves. Shuffles itself back if milled, limiting reanimator lines — best in sneak-show.

Emry, Lurker of the Loch.name: Emry, Lurker of the Loch
Emry, Lurker of the Loch.notes: Costs as little as {U} in artifact-heavy decks, mills four on entry, and recasts artifacts from the graveyard. A powerful engine piece that signals artifacts is open. Pairs exceptionally with free/cheap artifacts and self-mill loops.

Endurance.name: Endurance
Endurance.notes: Flash + reach body that doubles as a free graveyard hate spell via evoke. Shuts down reanimator, dredge, and flashback strategies instantly. The 3/4 reach body is relevant against flying threats; broadly powerful in any green deck.

Enduring Innocence.name: Enduring Innocence
Enduring Innocence.notes: The draw trigger only fires once per turn but repeatedly rewards playing small creatures. The death-to-enchantment clause makes it nearly impossible to permanently remove, giving it long-term resilience. Best in white weenie shells where small creatures flood the board.

Entomb.name: Entomb
Entomb.notes: A one-mana instant that puts any card directly in the graveyard is essentially a tutor for reanimator strategies. Fetching Griselbrand or Iona at instant speed on the opponent's end step is game-defining.

Ephemerate.name: Ephemerate
Ephemerate.notes: Rebound makes this effectively two blinks for one mana, generating enormous value with any ETB creature. Protects key threats from removal and resets ETBs. Synergizes deeply with value creatures; can generate near-infinite loops with Eternal Witness.

Esika's Chariot.name: Esika's Chariot
Esika's Chariot.notes: Immediately creates two 2/2s on ETB, then exponentially copies whatever your best token is each attack. Especially broken in token strategies — copying a Wurm or Treasure token is often game-ending. Crew 4 is steep but you usually don't need to crew it.

Etali, Primal Conqueror // Etali, Primal.name: Etali, Primal Conqueror // Etali, Primal Sickness
Etali, Primal Conqueror // Etali, Primal.notes: A 7-mana hard cast is viable in ramp, but Etali truly shines when cheated in via Sneak Attack or Show and Tell — the ETB trigger alone can win the game by casting multiple opponent spells for free. The transform ability is a largely irrelevant bonus in cube.

Eternal Witness.name: Eternal Witness
Eternal Witness.notes: One of the most versatile value creatures in the cube. Recovers any card — a Time Walk loop with Ephemerate is a near-lock. Works in virtually any green deck and enables powerful graveyard recursion loops.

Everflowing Chalice.name: Everflowing Chalice
Everflowing Chalice.notes: Scales with available mana — kicked twice becomes a reliable 4-mana rock producing 2 per turn. Works well with Voltaic Key and artifact synergy decks. A bit slow compared to Mox/Signets but flexible and colorless.

Exhume.name: Exhume
Exhume.notes: Two-mana symmetrical reanimate is a staple in reanimator strategies. The symmetry is rarely a downside if you set up the graveyard correctly. Pairs perfectly with Entomb on turn 1 into Exhume on turn 2 for a turn-2 Griselbrand.

Expedition Map.name: Expedition Map
Expedition Map.notes: Colorless land tutor that fetches any land — particularly valuable for finding Tolarian Academy, Dark Depths, or utility lands. Slow at 3 total mana to activate, but the flexibility across all archetypes gives it consistent value.

Exploration.name: Exploration
Exploration.notes: A one-mana enchantment that accelerates your land drops is powerful in any green deck but truly broken in Lands strategies — combining with Crucible of Worlds and Wrenn and Six to replay fetchlands every turn for repeated value and ramp.

Expressive Iteration.name: Expressive Iteration
Expressive Iteration.notes: Arguably the best two-mana draw spell for tempo decks. Filtering three cards and effectively drawing two while giving a one-turn window to play the exiled card is exceptional card selection. A staple in any UR shell.

Fable of the Mirror-Breaker // Reflectio.name: Fable of the Mirror-Breaker // Reflection of Kiki-Jiki
Fable of the Mirror-Breaker // Reflectio.notes: Does everything: ramps with Treasure, filters your hand, then transforms into a Kiki-Jiki effect that can go infinite with Pestermite or Zealous Conscripts. Chapter II looting also serves reanimator/storm set-up. One of the most multi-functional red cards in the cube.

Fabled Passage.name: Fabled Passage
Fabled Passage.notes: Generic mana-fixing land that fits any multicolor deck. Notably synergizes with landfall and land-recursion effects. Empty archetypes reflects universal playability.

Faerie Mastermind.name: Faerie Mastermind
Faerie Mastermind.notes: Flash flyer that generates card advantage against draw-heavy opponents; fits tempo and control shells well. The active ability can also be used politically or to fuel card draw engines.

Faithless Looting.name: Faithless Looting
Faithless Looting.notes: One of the best enablers in graveyard and combo strategies. Flashback makes it a two-for-one in terms of looting over the course of the game. Fits any deck that wants to pitch cards to the graveyard.

Fallen Shinobi.name: Fallen Shinobi
Fallen Shinobi.notes: Ninjutsu cost makes it easy to cheat in behind any evasive creature. The free-cast effect from combat damage can be game-winning, but requiring an unblocked attacker is a meaningful condition.

Fanatic of Rhonas.name: Fanatic of Rhonas
Fanatic of Rhonas.notes: Ferocious mode generates massive mana but requires a 4-power creature already on board. Eternalize gives it late-game relevance. Best in ramp/stompy decks that reliably hit power-4 creatures.

Fastbond.name: Fastbond
Fastbond.notes: Extremely powerful with land-recursion loops (Crucible + fetch lands, Loam). Enables explosive mana development. The life loss is largely irrelevant if you're gaining value per land drop. A strong signal for the Lands archetype.

Fatal Push.name: Fatal Push
Fatal Push.notes: One of the best black removal spells in the format. Revolt is easy to trigger with fetchlands. Hits the vast majority of threats in the cube at one mana.

Fear of Missing Out.name: Fear of Missing Out
Fear of Missing Out.notes: The looting ETB fuels graveyard strategies. Delirium-triggered extra combat is a strong upside in aggressive or graveyard-heavy decks. Requires setup but can be a serious threat in the right shell.

Fiery Confluence.name: Fiery Confluence
Fiery Confluence.notes: Extremely flexible modal spell. Three modes of 2 damage to each opponent is a near-Lightning Bolt to the face; triple 1-damage sweeper kills x/1s; can also destroy up to three artifacts. Modal flexibility makes it consistently strong.

Figure of Destiny.name: Figure of Destiny
Figure of Destiny.notes: A one-drop that scales into a late-game threat. Investing mana over multiple turns is a real cost in Vintage Cube, but the payoff is substantial. Best in aggressive RW or mono-red/white decks.

Fire Covenant.name: Fire Covenant
Fire Covenant.notes: One of the best wraths in the format for dealing with swarms of creatures. Instant speed and scalable damage distribution make it exceptional. Life payment is largely irrelevant in control/midrange decks.

Fire Nation Occupation.name: Fire Nation Occupation
Fire Nation Occupation.notes: Creates tokens both on ETB and whenever you cast spells on opponents' turns — strong synergy with flash/counterspell-heavy control decks. The firebending mana can help cast spells during the opponent's turn. A build-around that rewards reactive strategies.

Fireblast.name: Fireblast
Fireblast.notes: The free mode (sacrificing two Mountains) is the main appeal — it closes games from hand at zero mana, making it exceptional in mono-red burn shells where you're happy to sacrifice Mountains in the late game.

Firebolt.name: Firebolt
Firebolt.notes: Two-damage for 1 mana is below the curve of Lightning Bolt, but flashback gives it late-game reach. Solid in burn/aggro decks that want to maximise the number of damage spells.

Flame of Anor.name: Flame of Anor
Flame of Anor.notes: Wizard synergy is the key upside — in an Izzet deck with Snapcaster Mage or similar Wizards, getting two modes (e.g. draw 2 + destroy artifact or 5 damage) on an instant is excellent value. Without a Wizard it is still a versatile modal spell.

Flame Slash.name: Flame Slash
Flame Slash.notes: Hits creatures only and is a sorcery, but 4 damage for 1 mana is exceptional efficiency for killing large creatures. Notably misses players, limiting its burn-finisher role.

Flash.name: Flash
Flash.notes: Flash + Protean Hulk is a classic two-card instant-win combo. Even without Hulk it can cheat in fatties at instant speed, making it a strong signal for cheat-into-play strategies.

Flickerwisp.name: Flickerwisp
Flickerwisp.notes: Its ETB exile effect hits any permanent — it can blink your own ETB creatures, reset opposing planeswalkers temporarily, or interact with Smokestack/Tangle Wire counters in stax builds.

Flooded Strand.name: Flooded Strand
Flooded Strand.notes: A format staple fetch land. Enables Brainstorm shuffling and fixes mana for any white/blue deck. Archetypes left empty because it is generically good across virtually every blue and/or white archetype.

Force of Negation.name: Force of Negation
Force of Negation.notes: Free counter on your opponent's turn is format-defining protection. Exiles rather than counters, so it beats graveyard recursion and dodges Cavern of Souls effects. Pairs especially well with other free interaction.

Force of Vigor.name: Force of Vigor
Force of Vigor.notes: Free artifact/enchantment hate on the opponent's turn is extremely powerful in Vintage Cube where fast mana artifacts are everywhere. Two-for-one removal for zero mana is a massive tempo swing.

Force of Will.name: Force of Will
Force of Will.notes: The defining blue permission spell in Vintage Cube. Nearly every blue deck wants it. Counters anything for free at the cost of card disadvantage — the safety valve that keeps degenerate strategies honest.

Forensic Gadgeteer.name: Forensic Gadgeteer
Forensic Gadgeteer.notes: Cost reduction on artifact activated abilities and free Clue generation on artifact casts can be powerful in a dedicated artifacts shell, but it is a 3-mana 3/2 with no immediate impact — too slow and narrow outside that context.

Forth Eorlingas!.name: Forth Eorlingas!
Forth Eorlingas!.notes: A flexible token spell that also grants monarch, providing ongoing card advantage. The trample + haste riders make the tokens immediately relevant. Best in aggressive token strategies that can pressure opponents the same turn.

Fractured Identity.name: Fractured Identity
Fractured Identity.notes: A unique exile-based removal spell that gives opponents a copy — best used against your own bomby permanents (Blightsteel, Emrakul) since the token copies go to all OTHER players, which in a multiplayer sense is fine, and in 1v1 you are giving them the copy. Most powerful when used on something symmetrically bad for the opponent or something you control that's already triggering a 'when it enters' effect. In Vintage Cube 1v1, it's primarily elite removal for indestructible/hexproof permanents at the cost of giving the opponent a token copy.

Frantic Search.name: Frantic Search
Frantic Search.notes: Untapping three lands makes this effectively free or even mana-positive with Tolarian Academy or other mana-producing lands. A staple in Storm for chaining spells and in Reanimator/Dredge for filling the graveyard efficiently.

Fury.name: Fury
Fury.notes: Evoke mode acts as a free Flame Slash-style sweeper that can clear multiple small creatures or a planeswalker. In fair decks it's a tempo blowout; in Sneak Attack / reanimator it's a powerful ETB creature. Synergizes with blink effects for repeated ETB triggers.

Gaea's Cradle.name: Gaea's Cradle
Gaea's Cradle.notes: Produces obscene amounts of green mana with any creature-heavy board. A signpost for token and ramp strategies. Pairs devastatingly with Craterhoof Behemoth as a finisher.

Galvanic Discharge.name: Galvanic Discharge
Galvanic Discharge.notes: A flexible removal spell that scales with energy. The three free energy counters often make it a 3-damage removal spell at instant speed for {R}, which is excellent. It can grow larger if you've built up energy from other sources, though energy synergies in Vintage Cube are limited.

Gau, Feral Youth.name: Gau, Feral Youth
Gau, Feral Youth.notes: The damage trigger requires a card to leave your graveyard this turn, which demands some self-mill or cycling support that red aggro doesn't naturally run. The attack-triggered counter growth is fine but the overall package is niche and inconsistent without graveyard-interaction support.

Generous Ent.name: Generous Ent
Generous Ent.notes: The body is overcosted for Vintage Cube. Its primary value is the Forestcycling ability, which can fetch a basic Forest to enable ramp decks, but even then it's a weak inclusion. The Food token is a minor life gain bonus but doesn't make up for the overall inefficiency.

Generous Plunderer.name: Generous Plunderer
Generous Plunderer.notes: The Treasure-gifting upkeep ability is a political/symmetrical effect that can backfire by giving opponents mana. The attack damage trigger scales with opponent artifacts but requires setup. Most interesting in artifact-heavy metas but generally outclassed by other 2-drops.

Get Lost.name: Get Lost
Get Lost.notes: Hits creatures, enchantments, AND planeswalkers at instant speed for {1}{W} — an incredibly efficient and versatile answer. The Map tokens given to the opponent are a real downside but rarely outweigh the value of the removal. Competes with Swords to Plowshares and Path to Exile as white's premier removal.

Gitaxian Probe.name: Gitaxian Probe
Gitaxian Probe.notes: Essentially a free cantrip that also gives perfect information. Invaluable in Storm for storm count and in combo decks for knowing when it's safe to go off. The life payment is almost always correct. A format staple in any blue combo or tempo deck.

Giver of Runes.name: Giver of Runes
Giver of Runes.notes: A functional upgrade to Mother of Runes in most situations — can protect itself from colored threats and grants protection to other creatures. Enables aggressive creature-based strategies by keeping key threats safe. Slightly worse than Mother of Runes against colorless threats on itself.

Glimmer Lens.name: Glimmer Lens
Glimmer Lens.notes: The For Mirrodin! rider gives a free 2/2 Rebel token and the card draw trigger fires whenever the equipped creature attacks alongside another creature. In a wide white aggro deck this provides card advantage, but the equip cost and overall setup make it slow for Vintage Cube. The free token on entry adds some immediate board presence.

Glorybringer.name: Glorybringer
Glorybringer.notes: 5-mana Dragon with haste and a built-in removal trigger on exert makes it a strong aggressive and midrange finisher. The removal-on-attack clause is particularly relevant against creature-based strategies.

Goblin Bombardment.name: Goblin Bombardment
Goblin Bombardment.notes: Free sacrifice outlet that converts creature damage into reach. Particularly powerful as a combo finisher in Kiki-twin (sac infinite tokens for lethal). Also protects creatures from exile-based removal.

Godless Shrine.name: Godless Shrine
Godless Shrine.notes: Dual land staple. Enables WB and WUB (Esper) strategies. Always a high pick for fixing — power tier reflects its role as premier mana fixing rather than standalone power.

Goldspan Dragon.name: Goldspan Dragon
Goldspan Dragon.notes: Generates Treasures on attack and when targeted by spells, making it both a threat and a mana accelerant. The double-mana Treasures can enable explosive follow-up turns or fuel storm-style plays.

Grave Titan.name: Grave Titan
Grave Titan.notes: One of the best reanimator targets in the cube: immediate token production on ETB means it generates value even against bounce, and deathtouch makes combat miserable for opponents.

Greasewrench Goblin.name: Greasewrench Goblin
Greasewrench Goblin.notes: Exhaust looting effect is one-time only, limiting long-term value. Best in decks that want to fill the graveyard quickly (storm, breach) or pair with a 1-drop goblin synergy shell.

Great Divide Guide.name: Great Divide Guide
Great Divide Guide.notes: Provides any-color mana fixing to lands and Allies you control. Relatively narrow — needs a critical mass of Ally cards or multicolor land bases to shine. The fixing can be excellent in the right multicolor shell.

Green Sun's Zenith.name: Green Sun's Zenith
Green Sun's Zenith.notes: Extremely flexible green tutor that scales with the game. At X=1 it fetches Dryad Arbor as a mana fix; at higher values it grabs any green bomb. Reshuffling itself prevents decking. A cornerstone of green-based midrange and ramp.

Grief.name: Grief
Grief.notes: Evoke for free discard on turn 1 is extremely powerful disruption. In reanimator, reanimate the evoked Grief immediately for repeated hand disruption with a 3/2 menace. Devastating in any black strategy that can exploit its evoke.

Grim Lavamancer.name: Grim Lavamancer
Grim Lavamancer.notes: Repeatable 2-damage pinger that rewards fetchlands and spell-heavy decks filling the graveyard. Excellent at picking off small creatures and dealing reach damage. Gets better as the game goes long.

Grim Monolith.name: Grim Monolith
Grim Monolith.notes: Produces 3 colorless mana immediately for 2. With Power Artifact, generates infinite colorless mana — a two-card combo that wins on the spot in many shells. Foundational fast mana piece in storm, tinker, and stax.

Griselbrand.name: Griselbrand
Griselbrand.notes: The premier reanimator target. Drawing 7 cards immediately upon cheating it into play is often game-winning, and the flying lifelink body stabilizes most board states. Sneak Attack + Griselbrand draws 14 cards before the end step.

Grist, the Hunger Tide.name: Grist, the Hunger Tide
Grist, the Hunger Tide.notes: Uniquely a creature while not on the battlefield, making it tutorable with creature tutors like Survival of the Fittest. The +1 mills and generates tokens, synergizing heavily with graveyard strategies. Extremely versatile removal on -2 and a potential game-ending -5 in creature-heavy decks.

Guardian Scalelord.name: Guardian Scalelord
Guardian Scalelord.notes: The Backup 1 gives flying to another creature and the attack trigger recurs permanents from the graveyard — but at 5 mana as a vanilla 4/4 when not attacking, it's slow and niche. The recursion clause is powerful if you can buff its power, but it's too conditional for most cubes.

Guide of Souls.name: Guide of Souls
Guide of Souls.notes: A 1-mana creature that rewards going wide — accumulates energy quickly in token strategies and can randomly upgrade an attacker into a flying Angel. Life gain is a minor bonus. Best in token-heavy white decks where creatures enter frequently.

Gush.name: Gush
Gush.notes: Effectively a free cantrip in many situations — returning two Islands is minimal cost in blue decks. Broken with Fastbond. Excellent storm enabler (fuels spell count and storm count), and great in tempo decks that want card advantage without spending mana on their opponent's turn.

Gut, True Soul Zealot.name: Gut, True Soul Zealot
Gut, True Soul Zealot.notes: Sacrificing artifacts or expendable creatures to generate 4/1 menace Skeleton tokens is a great rate. Naturally fits into artifact sacrifice synergies and aggressive red/black shells. The Background clause is irrelevant in Vintage Cube.

Hallowed Fountain.name: Hallowed Fountain
Hallowed Fountain.notes: A dual-type fetchable dual land. Format staple for any blue-white strategy. High power purely due to mana-fixing role in a fetch-heavy format.

Harvester of Misery.name: Harvester of Misery
Harvester of Misery.notes: A sweeper-on-a-stick that also has a discard activation for emergency removal. Menace makes it decent in combat. The enter trigger wiping opposing small creatures is powerful but at 5 mana it competes with much stronger black finishers. Best as a reanimation target where the ETB is free.

Haywire Mite.name: Haywire Mite
Haywire Mite.notes: Efficient hate piece against artifacts and enchantments that also replaces itself with 2 life on death. Colorless-castable but green-activated, making it slightly awkward outside green. Excellent sideboard-level main deck inclusion; also synergizes with sacrifice and artifact recursion loops.

Headliner Scarlett.name: Headliner Scarlett
Headliner Scarlett.notes: Haste plus a forced unblockable alpha strike effect on entry is strong in red aggro. The upkeep impulse draw provides sustained card advantage, making this more than a one-dimensional beater. Four mana is on the high end for red aggro but the total package is solid.

Hedge Maze.name: Hedge Maze
Hedge Maze.notes: Enters tapped, which is a real drawback, but the Surveil 1 on entry provides meaningful library manipulation. Pairs extremely well with Brainstorm-style effects to set up or filter away bad draws. A solid green-blue dual for decks that want to set up their draw step.

Hexdrinker.name: Hexdrinker
Hexdrinker.notes: A 1-mana threat that scales into a near-unkillable 6/6 with protection from everything given enough mana investment. Excellent in green ramp shells where leveling happens quickly. Weak early if the game is fast, but a powerful mana sink in longer games. Better in powered cube where mana is plentiful.

Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis.name: Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis
Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis.notes: Essentially a free 8/8 trampler that casts itself from the graveyard. Requires significant graveyard setup via convoke and delve, making it a strong but build-around card. A true signpost for dredge strategies — seeing Hogaak signals you should be milling aggressively and building around graveyard recursion.

Horizon Canopy.name: Horizon Canopy
Horizon Canopy.notes: Comes down untapped in aggro/midrange and replaces itself late game. The life-payment dual is excellent in GW-adjacent strategies. Crucible/Loam synergies let you recur the draw ability.

Hullbreacher.name: Hullbreacher
Hullbreacher.notes: Flash makes this a blowout against Brainstorm, Ancestral Recall, and wheel effects. Paired with wheels, it converts the opponent's draws into your Treasure. Narset+Hullbreacher is a nearly lock-tight draw denial package.

Hymn to Tourach.name: Hymn to Tourach
Hymn to Tourach.notes: Two random discards on turn 2 is brutally powerful. Best in black-heavy discard suites alongside targeted discard spells for maximum hand destruction.

Icetill Explorer.name: Icetill Explorer
Icetill Explorer.notes: The combination of extra land drops, playing lands from the graveyard, and landfall milling is tailor-made for the Lands archetype. Mills to feed Loam or enable self-mill synergies.

Ignoble Hierarch.name: Ignoble Hierarch
Ignoble Hierarch.notes: The Jund-colored Birds of Paradise with Exalted. Fixes mana for BRG strategies and provides an extra +1/+1 in go-wide attackers or one-creature attacks. Slightly narrower than Noble Hierarch but similarly powerful in its colors.

Imperial Seal.name: Imperial Seal
Imperial Seal.notes: Essentially a black Vampiric Tutor at the same cost. Any black combo or control deck wants this. The sorcery speed is the only real downside versus Vampiric Tutor.

Indatha Triome.name: Indatha Triome
Indatha Triome.notes: Tapped tri-land primarily for BGW strategies. The cycling clause adds value in slower control or midrange decks. Fetchable type line (Plains/Swamp/Forest) adds meaningful flexibility with fetches.

Infernal Grasp.name: Infernal Grasp
Infernal Grasp.notes: Unconditional instant-speed destroy at 2 mana is excellent. The 2 life is rarely relevant; black midrange/control decks will gladly pay it. Notably handles indestructible-adjacent threats that exile-based removal misses.

Inquisition of Kozilek.name: Inquisition of Kozilek
Inquisition of Kozilek.notes: Hits the vast majority of relevant cards in the cube (most threats/spells are 3 MV or less). Pairs excellently with Thoughtseize for a complete turn-1 disruption package. Slightly narrower than Thoughtseize but no life loss.

Inspiring Vantage.name: Inspiring Vantage
Inspiring Vantage.notes: Fastland for RW: comes in untapped turns 1–3 (the turns that matter most for aggro), tapped thereafter. Best in aggressive strategies where the early game is critical.

Inti, Seneschal of the Sun.name: Inti, Seneschal of the Sun
Inti, Seneschal of the Sun.notes: Functions as a looting engine that rewards attacking. The exile-and-play effect turns discards into card advantage. Pairs well with discard payoffs (Hollow One) and wheels. Trample grant on an attacker is relevant pressure.

Ivora, Insatiable Heir.name: Ivora, Insatiable Heir
Ivora, Insatiable Heir.notes: Creates Blood tokens on entry and combat damage, which fuel discarding for +1/+1 counters. A self-contained discard/loot engine that grows naturally. Works well alongside other discard payoffs. Blood tokens also enable reanimator setups by discarding fatties.

Jace, the Mind Sculptor.name: Jace, the Mind Sculptor
Jace, the Mind Sculptor.notes: One of the most powerful planeswalkers ever printed. The 0 Brainstorm ability synergizes enormously with fetchlands. Near-ubiquitous in any blue control or tempo shell. Bounce and library manipulation make it a format-defining threat.

Jace, Vryn's Prodigy // Jace, Telepath U.name: Jace, Vryn's Prodigy // Jace, Telepath Unbound
Jace, Vryn's Prodigy // Jace, Telepath U.notes: Front side loots efficiently to fill the graveyard; flipped side recasts spells from the yard. Excellent in spell-heavy blue decks and especially powerful with premium instants/sorceries like Time Walk.

Jace, Wielder of Mysteries.name: Jace, Wielder of Mysteries
Jace, Wielder of Mysteries.notes: Functions as a secondary Laboratory Maniac win condition. The +1 mills opponents and replaces itself. Mostly relevant as a combo kill condition paired with self-mill effects; as a standalone planeswalker it's somewhat expensive at 4 mana.

Jacked Rabbit.name: Jacked Rabbit
Jacked Rabbit.notes: Scales with mana investment and generates tokens equal to its power. Best in token-anthem decks but requires meaningful X investment to be impactful. Ravenous card draw at X=5+ is a nice upside.

Jetmir's Garden.name: Jetmir's Garden
Jetmir's Garden.notes: Enters tapped which is a real cost in aggressive Naya shells, but fixing three colors is significant. Cycling for 3 is a safety valve. Solid in any Naya (RGW) deck.

Kaito, Bane of Nightmares.name: Kaito, Bane of Nightmares
Kaito, Bane of Nightmares.notes: Unique ninjutsu planeswalker that becomes a hexproof creature during your turn. The emblem +1 stacks and the tap/stun -2 provides tempo. Best in UB tempo/ninja builds or UB control. Requires creatures to attack with for the ninjutsu.

Kaldra Compleat.name: Kaldra Compleat
Kaldra Compleat.notes: Primarily a Stoneforge Mystic target or Tinker/cheat target. Living weapon means it arrives as a 5/5 first strike, trample, indestructible, haste that exiles blockers — extremely difficult to beat in combat. The equip cost is irrelevant when cheated out.

Kappa Cannoneer.name: Kappa Cannoneer
Kappa Cannoneer.notes: Improvise makes this potentially very cheap in artifact-heavy decks. Ward 4 makes it extremely difficult to remove. Grows quickly as artifacts ETB and repeatedly can't be blocked. Closes games fast in artifact shells.

Karakas.name: Karakas
Karakas.notes: Taps for white with a free, repeatable bounce effect on legendary creatures. Disrupts opposing legends and combos with your own ETB legends. Notably answers Show and Tell targets like Emrakul. No color identity restriction — works in any white deck.

Karn, Scion of Urza.name: Karn, Scion of Urza
Karn, Scion of Urza.notes: Colorless planeswalker that fits any deck but shines in artifacts where the -2 Construct token becomes enormous. The +1/-1 card selection loop provides persistent card advantage. A strong value engine in artifact-heavy lists.

Katara, Waterbending Master.name: Katara, Waterbending Master
Katara, Waterbending Master.notes: Rewards holding up countermagic and instant-speed interaction by accumulating experience counters. The draw/discard on attack is a solid loot effect that scales over time. Requires a reactive, instant-heavy game plan to maximize — niche but interesting in permission-heavy blue decks.

Keen-Eyed Curator.name: Keen-Eyed Curator
Keen-Eyed Curator.notes: Exiles graveyard cards one at a time; reaching 4+ card types for +4/+4 and trample is achievable in self-mill or graveyard-heavy metas. Acts as a graveyard hoser that can also become a large threat. Niche but has both disruption and payoff built in.

Ketria Triome.name: Ketria Triome
Ketria Triome.notes: Enters tapped is a real cost, but being fetchable and cycling for 3 makes it a flexible mana fixer for any GUR archetype. Especially valuable in multi-color decks that need all three colors or want a cycle-able land late.

Kitesail Freebooter.name: Kitesail Freebooter
Kitesail Freebooter.notes: A flying Tidehollow Sculler variant — combines disruption with a clock. Best in aggressive black shells that want to strip answers before pressing in with flyers.

Knight of the Reliquary.name: Knight of the Reliquary
Knight of the Reliquary.notes: Grows very large quickly in any deck running fetchlands. Can tutor Wasteland or Dark Depths repeatedly for a combo/utility axis. Extremely strong in lands-matter and midrange shells.

Kolaghan's Command.name: Kolaghan's Command
Kolaghan's Command.notes: Modal flexibility makes this one of the best BR spells. Recurring Snapcaster Mage while making an opponent discard is a two-for-one that's hard to match. The artifact destroy clause also punishes fast mana.

Laelia, the Blade Reforged.name: Laelia, the Blade Reforged
Laelia, the Blade Reforged.notes: Haste plus growing body plus card advantage engine — effectively gives red card selection while pressuring the board. Snowballs hard if unanswered. A rare red card that's both aggressive and grindy.

Lavaspur Boots.name: Lavaspur Boots
Lavaspur Boots.notes: Budget-ish haste enabler for Equipment synergy decks. Ward {1} provides some protection but the low stat bonus limits appeal outside of aggro or artifact synergy shells.

Ledger Shredder.name: Ledger Shredder
Ledger Shredder.notes: Powerful in spell-dense environments — grows every time any player casts a second spell, which happens constantly in Vintage Cube. Connive also filters hands and pitches cards for reanimator setups.

Legion Extruder.name: Legion Extruder
Legion Extruder.notes: The ETB 2 damage is a nice bonus, but the Golem-generation ability requires sacrificing other artifacts — best as a late-game engine in artifact decks with excess artifact fodder.

Leovold, Emissary of Trest.name: Leovold, Emissary of Trest
Leovold, Emissary of Trest.notes: Extremely punishing against card-draw-heavy blue strategies and targeted removal. Soft-locks opponents trying to draw multiple cantrips in a turn. A hallmark Sultai value piece that also signals that archetype when seen.

Leyline Binding.name: Leyline Binding
Leyline Binding.notes: In a fetchland-rich environment, this can reliably cost {1}{W} or even {W} with domain. Flash makes it a blowout in combat. Extremely efficient removal for enchantment-based control decks.

Library of Alexandria.name: Library of Alexandria
Library of Alexandria.notes: One of the most powerful lands ever printed. Drawing an extra card every turn from a land is format-warping. Colorless, so it fits into any deck, but requires careful hand management to trigger. Empty array for archetypes because it's universal colorless utility.

Life // Death.name: Life // Death
Life // Death.notes: Death half is a cheap reanimation spell that stacks well with Reanimate and Animate Dead. The Life half is mostly irrelevant in Cube but occasionally enables surprise attacks. Primarily drafted for Death in reanimator shells.

Lightning Bolt.name: Lightning Bolt
Lightning Bolt.notes: One of the most efficient spells in Magic. Hits creatures and players, making it universally playable in any red deck. Flashback via Snapcaster Mage is a well-known value line.

Lightning Greaves.name: Lightning Greaves
Lightning Greaves.notes: Equip {0} makes haste and shroud essentially free. Best with expensive finishers that need protection or haste enablement. Shroud can occasionally anti-synergize with your own targeting effects.

Liliana of the Veil.name: Liliana of the Veil
Liliana of the Veil.notes: Symmetrical discard is heavily asymmetric in practice — she pairs beautifully with recursive threats and reanimation targets. The -2 is a hard-to-answer removal spell on a planeswalker.

Lingering Souls.name: Lingering Souls
Lingering Souls.notes: Four flying power across two castings for {3}{W}{B} total is exceptional value. Slots into any white deck that can splash black for the flashback. Excellent Liliana of the Veil synergy — discard it, then flash it back.

Lion Sash.name: Lion Sash
Lion Sash.notes: Incidental graveyard hate stapled to a reasonable threat/equipment. The reconfigure ability provides flexibility between being a creature or a pump piece. Not a dedicated hate card but fills that role opportunistically.

Lion's Eye Diamond.name: Lion's Eye Diamond
Lion's Eye Diamond.notes: A functional Black Lotus in storm with Infernal Tutor (holding priority trick). Also enables fast reanimation and dredge by filling the graveyard. The 'discard hand' restriction is mitigated in these combo contexts.

Llanowar Elves.name: Llanowar Elves
Llanowar Elves.notes: Classic 1-mana dork. Accelerates into 3-drops on turn 2, which is impactful in cube. The body is relevant early but fragile. Best in green-based midrange and ramp strategies.

Loot, the Pathfinder.name: Loot, the Pathfinder
Loot, the Pathfinder.notes: Five mana is steep but the exhaust payoffs are massive: 3 mana, draw 3, or deal 3. Double strike, vigilance, and haste make it a legitimate combat threat too. Best cheated into play or cast in ramp/big-mana shells.

Loran of the Third Path.name: Loran of the Third Path
Loran of the Third Path.notes: ETB artifact/enchantment removal on a vigilant body is solid. The tap ability draws both players a card, which is a tempo loss unless used strategically. Blinkable for repeated removal. The group draw is a real downside in competitive cube.

Lórien Revealed.name: Lórien Revealed
Lórien Revealed.notes: The islandcycling mode for {1} is extremely flexible — it's essentially a dual-mode card that fixes mana early or draws 3 late. In control decks this rate is fine at full cost; the cycling makes it a near-free include.

Lose Focus.name: Lose Focus
Lose Focus.notes: Replicate makes this a storm enabler — each copy is a separate spell for storm count purposes. As a standalone counterspell it's weak (pay 2 to counter). Primarily a storm card; mediocre in normal control contexts.

Lotus Petal.name: Lotus Petal
Lotus Petal.notes: Free mana is always powerful in cube. Excellent in any combo deck needing to accelerate or hit a specific mana threshold. Counts as a spell for storm. Also serviceable in control as fast mana but most impactful in combo.

Luminarch Aspirant.name: Luminarch Aspirant
Luminarch Aspirant.notes: Steadily threatens to run away with the game if unanswered; the free +1/+1 counter each combat turn makes any creature a growing threat, rewarding aggressive white decks heavily.

Lurrus of the Dream-Den.name: Lurrus of the Dream-Den
Lurrus of the Dream-Den.notes: In a Vintage Cube context the Companion restriction is largely irrelevant since the cube is already full of cheap permanents. The recursive value of replaying 2-MV permanents each turn is extremely powerful, especially alongside cheap artifacts and disruptive creatures.

Lush Portico.name: Lush Portico
Lush Portico.notes: Enters tapped, so it's a significant tempo loss. The surveil 1 adds marginal value (smoothing draws, enabling light graveyard synergies) but doesn't compensate enough to make this competitive with untapped dual lands in the cube. Only worth considering in GW decks that can't access better fixing.

Lutri, the Spellchaser.name: Lutri, the Spellchaser
Lutri, the Spellchaser.notes: In Vintage Cube the Companion restriction (all unique card names) is trivially met since the cube itself has no duplicates, making Lutri a free extra card from outside the game. The copy effect on any instant/sorcery — especially Power cards — can be backbreaking. Mostly fits UR shells that are already full of instants and sorceries.

Magda, Brazen Outlaw.name: Magda, Brazen Outlaw
Magda, Brazen Outlaw.notes: The Treasure-to-tutor line is unrealistic in most games, but even as a 2/1 that generates Treasures whenever a Dwarf taps she provides real value in aggressive red or artifact-heavy shells. Treasure generation accelerates into other artifact payoffs.

Malcolm, Alluring Scoundrel.name: Malcolm, Alluring Scoundrel
Malcolm, Alluring Scoundrel.notes: Flash and flying make him easy to deploy and protect. The loot effect on each combat damage is solid card filtering; the four-counter payoff to cast discarded cards for free is a strong late-game ceiling. Best in UR/WU tempo where you can protect him and keep attacking.

Malevolent Rumble.name: Malevolent Rumble
Malevolent Rumble.notes: The combination of card selection (find any permanent), graveyard seeding, and a free Eldrazi Spawn mana accelerant makes this surprisingly efficient for green midrange and ramp decks. The spawn token bridges it into the next land drop or spell and the mill-to-graveyard aspect feeds reanimator/dredge incidentally.

Mana Confluence.name: Mana Confluence
Mana Confluence.notes: Staple rainbow fixing that slots into virtually every multicolor strategy. The life cost is negligible in most games but can be relevant against aggressive decks.

Mana Crypt.name: Mana Crypt
Mana Crypt.notes: Arguably the second-best mana rock in the cube after Sol Ring. The free {C}{C} per turn with only a coinflip downside is format-warping acceleration that enables broken turn-1 plays across every archetype.

Mana Drain.name: Mana Drain
Mana Drain.notes: The premier hard counter in the format. The mana conversion on resolution frequently funds a same-turn haymaker, making it format-defining. First-pickable in virtually any blue deck.

Mana Leak.name: Mana Leak
Mana Leak.notes: Reliable soft counter in the early game; falls off in the mid-to-late game when opponents can pay 3. Still a staple in blue control and tempo strategies that need countermagic depth.

Mana Tithe.name: Mana Tithe
Mana Tithe.notes: A surprise counterspell in white that catches opponents off guard, especially alongside other tax effects. Loses value quickly as games go long, but in a white weenie or stax shell where opponents are already mana-taxed it can be legitimately good.

Mana Vault.name: Mana Vault
Mana Vault.notes: One of the best fast-mana artifacts in the cube. The 1-damage-per-turn drawback is largely irrelevant when you're winning on turn 2-3. Pairs especially well with Manifold Key/Voltaic Key to untap it for free.

Manamorphose.name: Manamorphose
Manamorphose.notes: Essentially a free spell in Storm — it replaces itself, fixes mana, and increments the spell count. Narrow outside of Storm since it provides no tempo gain elsewhere.

Manifold Key.name: Manifold Key
Manifold Key.notes: Does double duty: untaps Time Vault or Mana Vault for value, and makes your Blightsteel or other fatties unblockable as a finisher. A strong artifact-archetype role-player.

Marauding Mako.name: Marauding Mako
Marauding Mako.notes: Grows large in discard-heavy shells but competes for space with better red threats. The cycling fallback is fine but not exciting. Primarily a niche payoff for heavy-discard builds.

Marsh Flats.name: Marsh Flats
Marsh Flats.notes: Generic WB fetchland that enables any white or black deck. Enables delirium/threshold and fuels Deathrite Shaman. No archetype-specific tag since it's universal fixing.

Master of Death.name: Master of Death
Master of Death.notes: The surveil-2 ETB mills key pieces into the graveyard for reanimator or dredge. The recurring-from-graveyard ability makes it a persistent threat and life-payment engine. Solid UB value creature.

Memory Jar.name: Memory Jar
Memory Jar.notes: A one-shot draw-7 that can instantly refuel Storm or enable a massive Tinker/Welder turn. Restricted in Vintage for good reason. Can end games on the spot when combined with fast mana.

Memory Lapse.name: Memory Lapse
Memory Lapse.notes: Effectively a hard counter in tempo decks — the opponent loses their next draw AND their mana. Especially brutal with Brainstorm or Jace to manipulate your own draws. Slightly weaker than true counters in pure control.

Metamorphosis Fanatic.name: Metamorphosis Fanatic
Metamorphosis Fanatic.notes: A Miracle reanimation effect that brings back a creature with lifelink — decent value but expensive at base cost. Best in a dedicated reanimator shell that can hit the miracle trigger. Too slow for most other strategies.

Meticulous Archive.name: Meticulous Archive
Meticulous Archive.notes: Enters tapped, which is a real cost, but the free surveil-1 on entry has strong synergy with Brainstorm — it can ship an unwanted card to the yard. Solid in WU shells that want both colors.

Mind Stone.name: Mind Stone
Mind Stone.notes: Versatile colorless accelerant that converts into a card draw in the late game. Fits any archetype needing early ramp, with the sacrifice outlet being especially useful in stax or artifact synergy decks.

Mind Twist.name: Mind Twist
Mind Twist.notes: With fast mana (Sol Ring, Mana Vault, Dark Ritual), this can empty an opponent's hand as early as turn 1. A devastating discard spell that warps games when cast for X=3 or more.

Mine Collapse.name: Mine Collapse
Mine Collapse.notes: Effectively a free 5-damage instant in mono-red if you sacrifice a Mountain — Fireblast-style cost reduction makes this punchy for aggro-burn. In other colors it costs 4 mana, making it much less impressive.

Minsc & Boo, Timeless Heroes.name: Minsc & Boo, Timeless Heroes
Minsc & Boo, Timeless Heroes.notes: Generates a free hasty creature every upkeep, pumps threats, and provides a sacrifice outlet that doubles as a Boo-fueled draw engine. Exceptional in Gruul/Jund midrange strategies.

Miscalculation.name: Miscalculation
Miscalculation.notes: A flexible early counterspell that cycles late. The cycling ability gives it non-negligible late-game utility, making it slightly better than a vanilla Mana Leak in long games.

Mishra's Bauble.name: Mishra's Bauble
Mishra's Bauble.notes: Free spell that replaces itself a turn later. Best in artifact synergy decks and storm (inflates spell count); also useful in dredge/graveyard strategies that want cards in the graveyard.

Mishra's Workshop.name: Mishra's Workshop
Mishra's Workshop.notes: One of the most powerful lands in the game for artifact-based strategies. Generating triple colorless for artifacts makes it a cornerstone of Workshop-Stax, artifact combo, and Tinker shells.

Misty Rainforest.name: Misty Rainforest
Misty Rainforest.notes: Universal blue/green fetch land. Pairs exceptionally with Brainstorm to shuffle away dead cards. Fits any deck running Islands or Forests, so no specific archetype tags — pure mana-fixing utility.

Mockingbird.name: Mockingbird
Mockingbird.notes: A highly flexible Clone variant that adds flying and can be cast for a cheap X to copy a relevant low-MV target, or large to copy a bomb. Works well in tempo and control shells that want adaptive threats.

Monstrous Rage.name: Monstrous Rage
Monstrous Rage.notes: One mana for +3/+1 and trample effectively (counting the Role token) is strong tempo. Excellent on evasive or prowess-style threats. The Role token has lasting value even after the spell resolves.

Monument to Endurance.name: Monument to Endurance
Monument to Endurance.notes: Colorless artifact that rewards discard synergies with three distinct modes. Especially powerful in reanimator and storm decks that loot/wheel frequently, providing card advantage, mana, and drain all in one.

Mother of Runes.name: Mother of Runes
Mother of Runes.notes: One of the best 1-drops in white. Protects key creatures from removal and grants evasion when attacking. A hallmark white weenie/Death and Taxes card that signals the archetype is open.

Mox Diamond.name: Mox Diamond
