Mox Diamond.notes: Free mana acceleration with a meaningful cost (discard a land). Pairs superbly with land recursion effects. Essential in fast combo and storm lists; the land discard is mitigated in Lands/Loam strategies.

Mox Emerald.name: Mox Emerald
Mox Emerald.notes: One of the five Power Nine Moxen. Free green mana on turn one is format-warping. Fits any green deck but is especially impactful in green-based ramp and storm lists that leverage fast mana.

Mox Jet.name: Mox Jet
Mox Jet.notes: One of the Power Nine. Free black mana accelerant that warps the early game. Fits any black deck and enables broken starts in storm/combo. Also a Tinker target body.

Mox Opal.name: Mox Opal
Mox Opal.notes: Requires metalcraft (3 artifacts), so it's meaningfully weaker than the Power Nine Moxen outside artifact-dense decks. A strong payoff in artifact strategies but merely decent in generic lists.

Mox Pearl.name: Mox Pearl
Mox Pearl.notes: One of the Power Nine. Free white mana accelerant that enables turn-one plays like Swords to Plowshares or Armageddon.

Mox Ruby.name: Mox Ruby
Mox Ruby.notes: One of the Power Nine. Free red mana accelerant enabling explosive aggro or combo openings.

Mox Sapphire.name: Mox Sapphire
Mox Sapphire.notes: Widely considered the most powerful of the five Moxen given blue's dominance. Enables turn-one Force of Will / Ancestral Recall and fuels every blue archetype.

Multiversal Passage.name: Multiversal Passage
Multiversal Passage.notes: Essentially a dual land (enters untapped for 2 life). Colorless identity means it slots into any multicolor deck needing fixing. The life payment keeps it from being S-tier, but it's a strong land.

Mutagenic Growth.name: Mutagenic Growth
Mutagenic Growth.notes: Zero-mana pump is most relevant in infect/prowess shells where free spells matter. In a typical cube it's narrow, but it can fuel storm spell count or protect a key attacker.

Mystic Confluence.name: Mystic Confluence
Mystic Confluence.notes: Extremely flexible modal instant. Drawing 3 cards at instant speed is often the best mode; mix-and-matching counter + bounce + draw makes it a swiss-army knife for control and tempo.

Mystical Tutor.name: Mystical Tutor
Mystical Tutor.notes: Instant-speed tutor for any instant or sorcery puts a live threat on top. Finding Time Walk, Ancestral Recall, or a combo piece for {U} is format-defining. Loses card advantage but the selection is enormous.

Nadu, Winged Wisdom.name: Nadu, Winged Wisdom
Nadu, Winged Wisdom.notes: Generates massive card advantage and land drops when opponents target your creatures or you equip/target them yourself. A signpost for a lands-based value engine; best with free-targeting effects like Equipment or Quirion Ranger.

Narset, Parter of Veils.name: Narset, Parter of Veils
Narset, Parter of Veils.notes: The static ability shuts down opposing Ancestral Recall, Brainstorm, and draw spells — backbreaking against blue decks. Pairs brutally with wheel effects to empty opponents' hands. Her -2 digs for combo/control pieces.

Natural Order.name: Natural Order
Natural Order.notes: Sacrifice a green creature to instantly deploy any green creature from your library — most often Craterhoof Behemoth for an immediate win or Primeval Titan for overwhelming advantage. Signposts a green stompy/ramp strategy.

Necromancy.name: Necromancy
Necromancy.notes: Instant-speed reanimation when cast at flash speed is very powerful; the sacrifice rider only applies if cast at instant speed, making it a free Animate Dead otherwise. Strong reanimator signpost but also slots into any black midrange/control shell with fatties.

Nethergoyf.name: Nethergoyf
Nethergoyf.notes: Grows large quickly in graveyard-heavy decks; escape mode provides excellent late-game resilience. Best in decks that naturally fill the yard with diverse card types.

Nettlecyst.name: Nettlecyst
Nettlecyst.notes: Living weapon means it enters with a free body; in a dense artifact/enchantment deck can produce a very large attacker. Particularly potent in stax/artifacts where the battlefield is cluttered with permanents.

Night's Whisper.name: Night's Whisper
Night's Whisper.notes: Efficient two-for-one at two mana; a black Divination that costs life instead of an extra mana. Sees play in storm as a cantrip density spell and in reanimator to find pieces.

Nissa, Who Shakes the World.name: Nissa, Who Shakes the World
Nissa, Who Shakes the World.notes: Doubling Forest mana is immediately impactful; the +1 creates a sizable threat. The ultimate is game-ending. Best in ramp shells with many Forests but powerful anywhere green.

No More Lies.name: No More Lies
No More Lies.notes: A Mana Leak variant that exiles rather than bouncing to hand, preventing graveyard shenanigans. Solid in any tempo or control shell with WU but not format-defining.

Noble Hierarch.name: Noble Hierarch
Noble Hierarch.notes: One of the best mana dorks in the cube: exalted provides a real combat bonus, and the three-color production (GWU) enables splashes across many archetypes.

Oath of Druids.name: Oath of Druids
Oath of Druids.notes: Classic Oath combo: pair with Forbidden Orchard to give the opponent a creature token, then trigger Oath to cheat a Griselbrand or Emrakul into play. Requires keeping your own creature count low.

Occult Epiphany.name: Occult Epiphany
Occult Epiphany.notes: Instant-speed looting at scale that also creates Spirit tokens based on card types discarded; great with token-payoff creatures in UR or WU. Can pitch lands/extra copies while building a board.

Ocelot Pride.name: Ocelot Pride
Ocelot Pride.notes: One-mana lifelinker that snowballs into a token army; the city's blessing mode doubles tokens created each turn, making it an exponential threat in white aggro or tokens.

Oko, Thief of Crowns.name: Oko, Thief of Crowns
Oko, Thief of Crowns.notes: Famously broken card; +2 creates a food token and +1 turns threats/mana rocks into 3/3 Elks, neutering opponents' key permanents. Fits into almost any GU shell and warps games around him.

Oliphaunt.name: Oliphaunt
Oliphaunt.notes: Mountaincycling for {1} is the main appeal — it's an efficient land-fetcher that can also serve as a late-game trampling beater. Mainly a mana fixer/redundancy piece in red decks, rarely hard-cast.

Omnath, Locus of Creation.name: Omnath, Locus of Creation
Omnath, Locus of Creation.notes: One of the most powerful 4-drops in the format. Draws a card on ETB, gains 4 life on first landfall, produces RGWU on second, and deals 4 damage to all opponents on third — incredible in any 4-color shell or dedicated lands decks. The triple-landfall payoff is easily triggered with fetchlands.

Once Upon a Time.name: Once Upon a Time
Once Upon a Time.notes: Free on the first spell of the game makes this essentially a freeroll cantrip that finds creatures or lands. Excellent in creature-heavy green decks to ensure early mana dorks or land drops.

Orcish Bowmasters.name: Orcish Bowmasters
Orcish Bowmasters.notes: Flash and immediate impact make this a premium black creature. Punishes the ubiquitous cantrips and draw spells of the Vintage Cube environment extremely hard. The amass trigger accumulates into a serious threat over time.

Orcish Lumberjack.name: Orcish Lumberjack
Orcish Lumberjack.notes: Effectively produces 3 mana at the cost of a Forest — explosive ramp in Gruul or lands strategies that can recur or replace Forests. Very narrow outside of those archetypes.

Otawara, Soaring City.name: Otawara, Soaring City
Otawara, Soaring City.notes: Essentially a free bounce spell stapled to a blue land. Near-zero opportunity cost in any blue deck — the channel cost is often 3U or even 2U with legendary creatures. Answers virtually anything permanently isn't covered by counterspells.

Otharri, Suns' Glory.name: Otharri, Suns' Glory
Otharri, Suns' Glory.notes: Creates an escalating army of tokens each attack and can recur itself from the graveyard. The experience counter scaling makes it formidable in a longer game, but the 5-mana cost is steep for aggro.

Ouroboroid.name: Ouroboroid
Ouroboroid.notes: Scales entirely off its own power, so it needs to be large to be impactful. At 4 mana it's a 1/1 on entry and too slow for most cube strategies without dedicated counter synergies.

Oust.name: Oust
Oust.notes: One-mana removal that doesn't permanently answer a threat, but the library shuffle and 3-life gain offset its downsides. Pairs nicely with library manipulation that benefits from opponents' shuffling effects.

Overgrown Tomb.name: Overgrown Tomb
Overgrown Tomb.notes: Dual land — near-automatic inclusion in any BG deck. The 2-life payment is trivial. Fetchable by all green and black fetches.

Overlord of the Balemurk.name: Overlord of the Balemurk
Overlord of the Balemurk.notes: The impending cost makes this playable early as a pseudo-enchantment that mills 4 every end step before becoming a creature. The reanimation trigger on attack or ETB gives it excellent value in graveyard-centric decks.

Overlord of the Mistmoors.name: Overlord of the Mistmoors
Overlord of the Mistmoors.notes: Impending cost of 2WW is very accessible for white token/aggro decks. Generates two 2/1 flying insects on ETB and each attack, making it a recurring air force factory. The 7-mana full-cost version is rarely relevant.

Palace Jailer.name: Palace Jailer
Palace Jailer.notes: Combines removal, card advantage via monarchy, and a reasonable 2/2 body. Monarch is a powerful persistent effect in a cube with few flying blockers. The exile effect is temporary but often enough to tempo out opponents.

Palantír of Orthanc.name: Palantír of Orthanc
Palantír of Orthanc.notes: A slow value engine that forces opponents into a lose-lose: either you draw cards or you mill+drain them. The mill mode scales up dangerously over time and has fringe synergy with dredge/reanimator self-mill strategies, but the 3-turn delay makes it a grind-game card.

Paradoxical Outcome.name: Paradoxical Outcome
Paradoxical Outcome.notes: A storm all-star: bouncing 0-cost artifacts (Moxen, Lotus Petal) refuels your hand while keeping mana up via replaying them. Also sees play in artifact-heavy shells. Signposts storm clearly when seen in a pack.

Parallax Wave.name: Parallax Wave
Parallax Wave.notes: Five exile activations over its life is massive tempo, and it combos with Opalescence (flicker creatures indefinitely by removing counters from itself and returning itself to exile loop). Also useful for protecting your own creatures or enabling ETB re-triggers in blink shells.

Party Thrasher.name: Party Thrasher
Party Thrasher.notes: The convoke rider on exiled noncreature spells is a niche but powerful bonus in token-heavy red shells. Its discard-and-exile impulse draw is the main draw, effectively giving you card selection every turn and enabling free spells from exile.

Path to Exile.name: Path to Exile
Path to Exile.notes: One of the cleanest creature removal spells in the format. The ramp drawback is real but acceptable at 1 mana. Staple in every white deck.

Pentad Prism.name: Pentad Prism
Pentad Prism.notes: Two charge counters on entry means two mana of any color across two uses — effectively a ritual when cast with colored mana. Works best as color-fixing ramp in storm and combo shells. Goes to 0 counters quickly, so it's not a long-term mana source.

Pest Infestation.name: Pest Infestation
Pest Infestation.notes: Scales very well with mana — at X=3 you're destroying three artifacts/enchantments AND making six tokens. Excellent sideboard-style maindeck card against artifact-heavy metas, and the token production has real payoff potential in token strategies.

Phantasmal Image.name: Phantasmal Image
Phantasmal Image.notes: 2 mana clone is format-breakingly efficient. Copies the best thing on the board — whether that's an opponent's Griselbrand or your own Snapcaster. The Illusion downside is largely irrelevant when you're copying something that will immediately win or stabilize the board.

Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd.name: Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd
Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd.notes: Flash gives excellent tempo. The blink-on-attack is repeatable ETB abuse and can reset your own Stoneforge, bounce opponent's threats, or grow Phelia rapidly. Very powerful 2-drop that rewards blink-heavy white builds.

Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury.name: Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury
Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury.notes: Escape is a natural graveyard synergy — discard it early with Looting, then escape for free damage+life. As a recurring Boros body that drains 3 and gains 3 each time it enters or attacks, it's a clock and a stabilizer. The initial sacrifice clause means it's a dead draw without graveyard setup unless you're escaping immediately.

Phyrexian Metamorph.name: Phyrexian Metamorph
Phyrexian Metamorph.notes: Can be cast for 3 life instead of blue mana and copies any artifact OR creature, making it far more flexible than most clones. The artifact subtype also makes it Tinker/Welder-able. One of the most versatile cards in the cube.

Pinnacle Emissary.name: Pinnacle Emissary
Pinnacle Emissary.notes: Generates artifact tokens whenever you cast artifact spells — powerful in an artifacts/affinity shell. The Warp ability provides long-term value by recurring itself and re-triggering on recast. The Drone tokens are relevant blockers and attackers in the air, synergizing with an artifact-dense strategy.

Plateau.name: Plateau
Plateau.notes: Dual land fixing for any RW or splash-white/red deck. Fetchable by all on-color fetches. Format staple for mana consistency.

Polluted Delta.name: Polluted Delta
Polluted Delta.notes: Colorless fetchland that primarily enables UB/Esper/Grixis/Sultai strategies. Generically powerful mana fixing for any deck needing Islands or Swamps. Not archetype-specific enough to tag narrowly.

Ponder.name: Ponder
Ponder.notes: One of the best cantrips in the format. Pairs extraordinarily well with fetchlands to shuffle away unwanted cards. Goes in virtually every blue deck.

Portable Hole.name: Portable Hole
Portable Hole.notes: Efficient 1-mana exile removal that hits most early-game threats and mana rocks. Vulnerable to artifact removal, but the efficiency is hard to ignore in white aggro and control shells.

Portal to Phyrexia.name: Portal to Phyrexia
Portal to Phyrexia.notes: 9 mana hard-cast cost makes it a cheat target exclusively. Enters wiping opponent's board and then reanimates a creature each upkeep — powerful if it survives. Best in Tinker/Sneak-Show/Reanimator as a haymaker target.

Preacher of the Schism.name: Preacher of the Schism
Preacher of the Schism.notes: Deathtouch makes it a good blocker/attacker. Generates tokens when attacking the life leader and draws cards when you're ahead on life — solid value engine in midrange or life-gain adjacent shells.

Preordain.name: Preordain
Preordain.notes: Slightly weaker than Ponder but still an excellent cantrip. Scry 2 before drawing provides great library manipulation. Goes in virtually every blue deck.

Prismatic Ending.name: Prismatic Ending
Prismatic Ending.notes: In a multicolor cube environment, Converge often hits 3+ colors easily, making this exile removal that scales up to mana value 3+. Extremely flexible white removal spell for any deck splashing multiple colors.

Prismatic Vista.name: Prismatic Vista
Prismatic Vista.notes: Colorless fetch that finds any basic land. Excellent mana fixing and synergizes with Brainstorm/Ponder shuffle effects and Deathrite Shaman. Goes in any deck.

Proft's Eidetic Memory.name: Proft's Eidetic Memory
Proft's Eidetic Memory.notes: The draw-a-card ETB is fine, but the combat pump ability requires drawing 2+ cards in a single turn and a creature to target. Slow and win-more in most blue decks; best when there's a creature-based blue deck that naturally draws multiple cards per turn.

Psychic Frog.name: Psychic Frog
Psychic Frog.notes: Combat damage triggers draw, and the discard-to-grow mechanic synergizes beautifully with reanimator/dredge fueling strategies. Can become a massive flying threat quickly. Very strong in blue-black tempo and graveyard-matters shells.

Pyrogoyf.name: Pyrogoyf
Pyrogoyf.notes: Power/toughness scales with card types in all graveyards like Tarmogoyf. The 'deals damage equal to power' trigger on entry is strong, acting as free removal or damage. Best in Jund/Grixis where graveyards fill up quickly.

Pyrokinesis.name: Pyrokinesis
Pyrokinesis.notes: The free cast by exiling a red card is the real selling point — effectively a free Forked Bolt on steroids against token swarms or small creatures. Best in red decks that naturally flood on red cards.

Quantum Riddler.name: Quantum Riddler
Quantum Riddler.notes: The empty-hand draw bonus is a cute built-in synergy with Necropotence and Griselbrand-style engines, but 5 mana for a 4/4 flyer with a conditional upside is only medium in the Cube. Warp gives some flexibility.

Questing Beast.name: Questing Beast
Questing Beast.notes: Vigilance + deathtouch + haste + pseudo-unblockable by small creatures makes this an absolute beating. The planeswalker damage clause is highly relevant in Cube where planeswalkers are everywhere.

Raffine's Tower.name: Raffine's Tower
Raffine's Tower.notes: Enters tapped, but cycling for 3 is a solid failsafe. Primarily a fixing land for Esper decks; the cycling makes it non-dead in the late game.

Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer.name: Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer
Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer.notes: One of the best 1-drops ever printed. Generates Treasure and card advantage on every hit; Dash makes it almost impossible to answer profitably. Fits into every red aggro and tempo shell.

Ranger-Captain of Eos.name: Ranger-Captain of Eos
Ranger-Captain of Eos.notes: Tutors for any 1-drop creature (Mother of Runes, Thalia, etc.) and the sacrifice ability to lock out noncreature spells for a turn can be backbreaking against combo or control.

Raucous Theater.name: Raucous Theater
Raucous Theater.notes: Enters tapped, but the free surveil 1 on entry provides minor graveyard setup, which is a small but real upside in Rakdos and Reanimator shells.

Raugrin Triome.name: Raugrin Triome
Raugrin Triome.notes: Three-color land for Jeskai shells. Enters tapped which is a real cost; cycling for 3 prevents it from being a total dead draw late. Primarily for fixing in UR/WU/Jeskai decks.

Razorverge Thicket.name: Razorverge Thicket
Razorverge Thicket.notes: Effectively an untapped dual in the early game (enters untapped with 2 or fewer lands, i.e., turns 1–3). Strong fixing for GW and any deck splashing green or white.

Reanimate.name: Reanimate
Reanimate.notes: The single most efficient reanimation spell in existence. One black mana to steal any creature from any graveyard — format-defining card in the Cube.

Recurring Nightmare.name: Recurring Nightmare
Recurring Nightmare.notes: Returns to hand rather than going to the graveyard, making it nearly impossible to permanently answer. Chains recurring ETB creatures or simply keeps replaying fatties. Fantastic in graveyard value decks.

Relic of Sauron.name: Relic of Sauron
Relic of Sauron.notes: Taps for two colored mana in UBR, making it excellent fixing and acceleration in Grixis shells. The loot effect is a nice bonus. Compares favorably to Coalition Relic in the right shell.

Remand.name: Remand
Remand.notes: A tempo classic — counters a spell and replaces itself while bouncing the threat back. Particularly potent in storm as a ritual-like effect when targeting your own spells to draw a card while advancing the turn count.

Reprieve.name: Reprieve
Reprieve.notes: White Remand analogue. Hits any spell (not just countered), so slightly different application — can save your own spell from being countered or bounce a blocker-targeting removal spell. Draws a card, making it tempo-neutral at worst.

Retrofitter Foundry.name: Retrofitter Foundry
Retrofitter Foundry.notes: Extremely powerful with untap effects and sacrifice outlets. Produces artifact tokens at three different power levels, synergizes deeply with Welder and sacrifice-based artifact engines. The Construct upgrade is a significant late-game mana sink.

Riverpyre Verge.name: Riverpyre Verge
Riverpyre Verge.notes: Conditional dual land that enters untapped if you control an Island or Mountain. Effectively a dual in any UR or multicolor deck that already has the relevant basic types or other dual lands.

Robber of the Rich.name: Robber of the Rich
Robber of the Rich.notes: Haste and reach on a 2/2 for two is solid. The card-theft ability provides card advantage in aggressive decks that typically run low on resources. Pairs well with other Rogues like Ragavan to enable the cast-from-exile clause more reliably.

Sacred Foundry.name: Sacred Foundry
Sacred Foundry.notes: Shock dual land. Format staple for any RW or three-color deck splashing red or white. Essentially mandatory first pick for Boros and related archetypes.

Saheeli, Sublime Artificer.name: Saheeli, Sublime Artificer
Saheeli, Sublime Artificer.notes: Generates an army of Servo tokens from noncreature spells — great in spell-dense UR tempo and storm shells. The -2 can copy a powerful artifact for a turn, enabling one-shot Blightsteel attacks or value plays.

Samwise the Stouthearted.name: Samwise the Stouthearted
Samwise the Stouthearted.notes: Flash 2/1 that recurs any permanent card that died this turn — great protection against removal or wraths. Fits well in white-based aggressive or midrange decks. The Ring tempt is a minor bonus.

Satyr Wayfinder.name: Satyr Wayfinder
Satyr Wayfinder.notes: Digs four cards deep and fills the graveyard while fetching a land. Strong in both reanimator (mills fatties) and dredge (enables graveyard setup). The land-to-hand effect provides mana consistency as a bonus.

Savai Triome.name: Savai Triome
Savai Triome.notes: Enters tapped but cycles for {3} if not needed. Provides white, black, and red fixing — relevant for Jund splashing white, Esper, or Mardu shells. The cycling clause gives it some late-game utility.

Savannah.name: Savannah
Savannah.notes: Original dual land. Enters untapped for free — a foundational mana fixer for any GW or multicolor deck touching both colors. Always a high pick.

Sazh's Chocobo.name: Sazh's Chocobo
Sazh's Chocobo.notes: A 1/1 for {G} that grows with landfall. Requires dedicated land-heavy or fetch-heavy builds to shine. Comparable to Steppe Lynx but in green; decent in ramp/landfall builds but too fragile and slow for most cube environments.

Scalding Tarn.name: Scalding Tarn
Scalding Tarn.notes: Fetches Island or Mountain, enabling blue and red decks. Also fuels graveyard-synergy cards like Deathrite Shaman and threshold effects.

Screaming Nemesis.name: Screaming Nemesis
Screaming Nemesis.notes: Haste and damage redirection make it a persistent threat. The life-gain denial rider is relevant against lifegain-heavy control decks. Somewhat unique effect but a bit mana-intensive for pure burn.

Scrubland.name: Scrubland
Scrubland.notes: Original dual land. Enters untapped, fetchable, and enables any WB or WUB archetype seamlessly.

Scythecat Cub.name: Scythecat Cub
Scythecat Cub.notes: Landfall trigger that doubles counters on the second activation each turn creates explosive growth in ramp/lands strategies with multiple land drops. Trample ensures the counters translate to damage.

Seachrome Coast.name: Seachrome Coast
Seachrome Coast.notes: Effective dual land for WU decks in the early game when entering untapped on turns 1-3. Falls off slightly in the late game.

Seasoned Pyromancer.name: Seasoned Pyromancer
Seasoned Pyromancer.notes: Provides card filtering, token generation, and a graveyard recursion mode. Excellent in aggressive and midrange red shells. The discard effect also fuels reanimator or madness synergies.

Securitron Squadron.name: Securitron Squadron
Securitron Squadron.notes: Squad cost is expensive, so this mostly functions as a 2-mana 1/2 vigilance with upside when you flood tokens elsewhere. The self-buffing ETB trigger rewards token-dense builds.

Sensei's Divining Top.name: Sensei's Divining Top
Sensei's Divining Top.notes: One of the most powerful library manipulation tools in the format. Generic enough for any blue or non-blue deck. Pairs especially absurdly with Counterbalance and fetchlands to soft-lock the opponent.

Sentinel of the Nameless City.name: Sentinel of the Nameless City
Sentinel of the Nameless City.notes: Vigilance plus repeated Map token generation provides card advantage through exploration. Solid value creature in midrange green decks.

Serra Paragon.name: Serra Paragon
Serra Paragon.notes: Recursive value engine that recycles lands and low-CMC permanents from the graveyard. Flying body is relevant. The 2-life gain trigger accumulates over time. Excellent in white control and white aggro midrange.

Shadowy Backstreet.name: Shadowy Backstreet
Shadowy Backstreet.notes: Enters tapped, which is a meaningful cost in a fast format. The surveil 1 is a minor upside, making it only suitable when you desperately need WB fixing or want to fuel graveyard synergies.

Shallow Grave.name: Shallow Grave
Shallow Grave.notes: Instant-speed reanimation is powerful but the exile-at-end-step clause limits it to attack-step haymakers. Best with ETB or haste creatures like Griselbrand. A clear signal that reanimator is open.

Shelldock Isle.name: Shelldock Isle
Shelldock Isle.notes: The 20-card threshold is hard to hit fairly in Vintage Cube without deliberate self-mill (e.g. Griselbrand activations, Dredge), so this land rewards specific graveyard/combo decks that can deplete libraries quickly. It can also hide a game-winning fatty for free.

Sheltering Landscape.name: Sheltering Landscape
Sheltering Landscape.notes: Fetchable tapped basics plus a tri-color cycling cost make this very slow. Its primary appeal is as a mana fixer / cycling outlet in three-color aggro/midrange, but the cost is high enough that it rarely makes the cut in a powered Vintage Cube.

Sheoldred, the Apocalypse.name: Sheoldred, the Apocalypse
Sheoldred, the Apocalypse.notes: Format-defining threat. The symmetrical life-drain punishes every draw step and collapses opponents using cantrips, wheels, or Brainstorm. Deathtouch makes it extremely hard to attack through. Best in controlling black shells that also draw lots of cards themselves.

Sheoldred's Edict.name: Sheoldred's Edict
Sheoldred's Edict.notes: Flexible instant-speed edict that hits creatures OR tokens OR planeswalkers — the three modes make it relevant in many matchups. Best when the opponent has a single must-kill permanent; opponents with multiple tokens can often dodge the creature mode. The planeswalker clause is a notable upside over Diabolic Edict.

Shifting Woodland.name: Shifting Woodland
Shifting Woodland.notes: Delirium activation is non-trivial to set up in a typical Vintage Cube draft, but in dedicated lands or graveyard strategies it can copy a powerful permanent for one turn — e.g. becoming Urza's Saga or Dark Depths for a surprise combo threat. Enters tapped without a Forest, so requires some setup.

Shorikai, Genesis Engine.name: Shorikai, Genesis Engine
Shorikai, Genesis Engine.notes: Generates a Pilot token each activation, providing both a crew-able body and looting for value. Very hard to attack through as a 8/8 Vehicle. Best in controlling WU builds that want a resilient card-advantage engine that isn't a creature (dodges sorcery-speed removal).

Show and Tell.name: Show and Tell
Show and Tell.notes: The eponymous half of Sneak and Show. Cheating Emrakul or Omniscience into play on turn 3 is often game over. The symmetry is rarely a problem in a dedicated combo shell since the opponent rarely holds an equivalent bomb.

Sink into Stupor // Soporific Springs.name: Sink into Stupor // Soporific Springs
Sink into Stupor // Soporific Springs.notes: A modal DFC: the front face is a tempo bounce at instant speed (hits spells on the stack too, which is unusual), and the back face is a near-painless blue dual. The flexibility of having a cheap blue land that also doubles as interaction makes this a solid role-player in any blue tempo or control deck.

Six.name: Six
Six.notes: Combines self-mill, land retrieval, and retrace from the graveyard into one creature. A graveyard-oriented green deck can leverage this heavily — mill lands into hand while replaying permanents from the yard. Synergizes especially well with Loam and Crucible strategies.

Skullclamp.name: Skullclamp
Skullclamp.notes: One of the most powerful draw engines in the format. Attaching to any 1-toughness creature or token draws two cards for 1 mana. Absolutely busted with token producers. Fits nearly any aggressive or token strategy and can even slot into artifact combo builds.

Skyclave Apparition.name: Skyclave Apparition
Skyclave Apparition.notes: Efficient 2/2 body that hard-exiles permanents with MV ≤ 4 — covering most of the relevant hate bears, planeswalkers, and utility creatures in the format. The token it creates on death is usually small enough not to matter. Blink synergy with Restoration Angel is a bonus.

Smuggler's Copter.name: Smuggler's Copter
Smuggler's Copter.notes: Crew 1 means almost any creature turns it on. Looting every attack or block filters draws and fills the graveyard, making it excellent in aggro, tempo, and artifact synergy decks alike. A colorless 3/3 flyer with built-in card selection is exceptional value for 2 mana.

Snapcaster Mage.name: Snapcaster Mage
Snapcaster Mage.notes: One of the most universally powerful blue creatures in the cube. Flashbacks any instant/sorcery in the yard, making it a 2-for-1 in virtually any blue shell. Especially strong with cheap cantrips, removal, and counterspells.

Sneak Attack.name: Sneak Attack
Sneak Attack.notes: The definitive signpost for sneak-show. Once resolved, repeatedly cheating in Emrakul or Griselbrand ends games immediately. Redundant with Show and Tell for the same plan.

Snuff Out.name: Snuff Out
Snuff Out.notes: Free spell when you control a Swamp is a significant upside. The life payment is real but often negligible early. Hits most creatures cleanly; the nonblack clause is occasionally relevant but rarely a problem in cube.

Sol Ring.name: Sol Ring
Sol Ring.notes: Colorless universal mana rock that goes into every single deck. Producing two colorless mana for one generic mana is format-warping. Not archetype-specific but synergizes especially well in artifact-heavy builds and stax.

Solitude.name: Solitude
Solitude.notes: Free Swords to Plowshares at instant speed via evoke makes this an incredible tempo play. Pairs extremely well with Ephemerate and Restoration Angel to re-trigger ETB. One of the best white cards in the format.

Soul-Guide Lantern.name: Soul-Guide Lantern
Soul-Guide Lantern.notes: Primarily a graveyard hate piece. The draw mode gives a small upside but mainly included for sideboard-style hate against reanimator, dredge, and escape/flashback strategies. Rarely a main-plan card.

Sowing Mycospawn.name: Sowing Mycospawn
Sowing Mycospawn.notes: Ramp on a body is solid. The kicked mode lets you fetch a land AND blow up an opponent's land, which is a strong tempo swing. Particularly valuable in lands-matter strategies as it can fetch Dark Depths or utility lands. Devoid is incidental in cube.

Spara's Headquarters.name: Spara's Headquarters
Spara's Headquarters.notes: Tri-land that enters tapped but provides excellent fixing for Bant and Esper strategies. Cycling for 3 is a late-game safety valve. Enters tapped is a real cost but the flexibility is worth it in three-color decks.

Spell Pierce.name: Spell Pierce
Spell Pierce.notes: One mana counterspell that covers most relevant non-creature threats in vintage cube (planeswalkers, combo pieces, wraths). Scales down in late game but is a powerhouse in tempo and combo shells where protecting your key pieces matters.

Spellseeker.name: Spellseeker
Spellseeker.notes: Tutor on a stick. Fetches Time Walk, Tinker, Brainstorm, Ponder, Counterspell, Dark Ritual, and more — basically every broken 2-or-less mana spell in the cube. Extremely high ceiling in combo and tempo decks.

Spirebluff Canal.name: Spirebluff Canal
Spirebluff Canal.notes: Fast land that enters untapped in the early game where it matters most. Strong in UR-based aggressive and combo decks. Slight downside in longer games but rarely relevant when you're curving out on turns 1-3.

Springheart Nantuko.name: Springheart Nantuko
Springheart Nantuko.notes: Landfall token generator that scales extremely well with fetchlands and land-drop synergies. Bestow mode lets it ride an existing threat. Can produce overwhelming board presence in a land-heavy or tokens shell. The 1/1 fallback when not attached is a nice floor.

Squee, Goblin Nabob.name: Squee, Goblin Nabob
Squee, Goblin Nabob.notes: Primarily a discard outlet fodder — his recursive ability makes him an inexhaustible discard target for Survival of the Fittest, Bazaar of Baghdad, and Welder loops. Niche but excellent in those specific shells.

Staff of the Storyteller.name: Staff of the Storyteller
Staff of the Storyteller.notes: Enters with a token and converts ongoing token production into card draw. Scales well in token-heavy builds; a bit slow in non-token decks where it sits as a 1/1 generator.

Starting Town.name: Starting Town
Starting Town.notes: Effectively a City of Brass that enters tapped past turn 3. Useful as fixing in multicolor decks but falls behind pain lands and shock lands in power. Generally outclassed by better dual lands in Vintage Cube.

Steam Vents.name: Steam Vents
Steam Vents.notes: Shock land — dual land quality fixing for any UR or UR-adjacent strategy. Fetchable and nearly always enters untapped.

Stern Scolding.name: Stern Scolding
Stern Scolding.notes: Cheap conditional counterspell. Hits the majority of small threats in the cube (mana dorks, hatebears, small tempo creatures). Excellent in tempo shells where one-mana interaction is at a premium.

Stock Up.name: Stock Up
Stock Up.notes: Impulse-style card selection at sorcery speed. Putting two cards into hand (not just exile) and bottoming the rest is strong. A solid blue draw spell, though sorcery speed keeps it from S/A.

Stomping Ground.name: Stomping Ground
Stomping Ground.notes: Shock land for RG and any RG-adjacent strategy. Fetchable and near-unconditionally enters untapped.

Stoneforge Mystic.name: Stoneforge Mystic
Stoneforge Mystic.notes: One of the most powerful white cards in the cube. Tutors for and cheats into play any equipment. Signposts a heavy-equipment / artifact sub-theme but slots into almost any white deck running relevant targets.

Stormchaser's Talent.name: Stormchaser's Talent
Stormchaser's Talent.notes: Level 1 gives a prowess token immediately. Level 3 creates a token engine with every instant/sorcery, which is extremely powerful in UR spell-heavy decks. Level 2 is also relevant as a Regrowth for spells.

Strip Mine.name: Strip Mine
Strip Mine.notes: Unconditional land destruction. In most decks it's a colorless land with a one-time nuke; in Stax and Lands it becomes a repeated engine with Crucible of Worlds/Loam.

Subtlety.name: Subtlety
Subtlety.notes: A member of the free Elemental cycle. Flash flash flying body with a powerful ETB that tempo-swings the opponent. The evoke mode is a free Delay on a creature/planeswalker spell; full casting is a strong threat. Pairs excellently with other 'free' elementals.

Sunbaked Canyon.name: Sunbaked Canyon
Sunbaked Canyon.notes: Horizon land for RW — taps for R or W with life loss, or sacrifices for a card draw. Excellent in aggressive RW builds that want to trade life for speed and refill in the late game.

Sundering Eruption // Volcanic Fissure.name: Sundering Eruption // Volcanic Fissure
Sundering Eruption // Volcanic Fissure.notes: The front half is a land destruction spell with a minor evasion rider — niche in cubes that run land-hate strategies. The back half is a slow dual land that costs 3 life to enter untapped, making it reasonable mana fixing in aggressive RX decks but strictly worse than fetchlands or shocklands.

Sunfall.name: Sunfall
Sunfall.notes: Five-mana exile-all-creatures is premium (dodges indestructible), and the Incubator token scales to board size. Competes with Wrath of God and Farewell but the exile clause and token upside make it a strong control sweeper. Token synergy is a minor bonus in token-go-wide lists.

Suplex.name: Suplex
Suplex.notes: The exile-instead-of-die clause is excellent graveyard hate against reanimator and dredge targets. The artifact removal mode gives rare flexibility for red. Solid modal removal spell that punches above its weight in the right meta.

Survival of the Fittest.name: Survival of the Fittest
Survival of the Fittest.notes: One of the most powerful green enchantments in the cube. Tutors any creature into hand at instant speed while loading the graveyard — a perfect engine for reanimator and midrange toolboxes. Seeing this is a strong signal that reanimator or BG graveyard strategies are available.

Sword of the Meek.name: Sword of the Meek
Sword of the Meek.notes: On its own it's a mediocre equipment, but the Thopter Foundry + Sword of the Meek combo creates infinite life and tokens with any floating mana. A signpost for artifact combo strategies whenever Thopter Foundry is also in the cube.

Swords to Plowshares.name: Swords to Plowshares
Swords to Plowshares.notes: The gold standard of white removal — one mana, instant, permanent exile. The life-gain downside is negligible. Fits every white deck and is a generic staple, hence no specific archetype tags.

Sylvan Caryatid.name: Sylvan Caryatid
Sylvan Caryatid.notes: Hexproof and defender make it a resilient mana dork that survives most removal. Not as explosive as Birds of Paradise or Llanowar Elves but much harder to kill. Especially good in midrange and ramp as a durable accelerant.

Sylvan Safekeeper.name: Sylvan Safekeeper
Sylvan Safekeeper.notes: One-mana 1/1 that protects key creatures by sacrificing lands — extremely powerful in Lands strategies where land recursion with Crucible or Loam makes the cost trivial. Also useful in midrange to protect a key threat.

Taiga.name: Taiga
Taiga.notes: Original dual land — enters untapped, fetched by all RG fetchlands. Format staple for any deck in RG colors. High power due to perfect fixing with no downside.

Talisman of Conviction.name: Talisman of Conviction
Talisman of Conviction.notes: Solid two-mana mana rock for RW decks. The 1 damage is largely irrelevant in most contexts. Accelerates into turn-3 four-drops reliably in aggro and stax shells.

Talisman of Creativity.name: Talisman of Creativity
Talisman of Creativity.notes: Two-mana UR rock. Most relevant in UR Tempo and artifact-heavy storm/tinker lists that want cheap mana acceleration and incidental artifact count.

Talisman of Curiosity.name: Talisman of Curiosity
Talisman of Curiosity.notes: Two-mana GU rock fitting Sultai and ramp strategies. Also an artifact for synergy-based decks. Slightly less impactful than talismans in more aggressive color pairs since GU tends toward value rather than speed.

Talisman of Dominance.name: Talisman of Dominance
Talisman of Dominance.notes: Efficient 2-mana rock in UB colors. The 1 life payment is largely irrelevant; it slots well into any UB or artifact-centric shell needing early mana.

Talisman of Progress.name: Talisman of Progress
Talisman of Progress.notes: Solid color-fixing rock for WU shells. Helps cast 5-mana planeswalkers like Teferi on curve.

Talon Gates of Madara.name: Talon Gates of Madara
Talon Gates of Madara.notes: The enter-the-battlefield phase-out effect can protect your own creatures or tempo-lock an opponent's threat. The ability to play from hand for {4} means it doesn't cost a land drop in a pinch. Niche but interesting in stax/tempo shells.

Tamiyo, Collector of Tales.name: Tamiyo, Collector of Tales
Tamiyo, Collector of Tales.notes: The +1 mills while tutoring, which is excellent in dredge/reanimator. The static immunity to discard and sacrifice is a nice bonus against disruption. The -3 recursion is very relevant in self-mill strategies.

Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student // Tamiyo, S.name: Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student // Tamiyo, Seasoned Scholar
Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student // Tamiyo, S.notes: A 1-mana flying threat that flips quickly in draw-heavy blue decks. The back face is a powerful planeswalker with a defensive +2 and strong ultimate. Easiest to flip in storm or cantrip-dense tempo decks where drawing 3 in a turn is routine.

Tarfire.name: Tarfire
Tarfire.notes: Strictly worse than Lightning Bolt except that being a Kindred Instant — Goblin means it's a Goblin card type in the graveyard, which pumps Tarmogoyf. Almost exclusively played to enable Tarmogoyf synergy.

Tarmogoyf.name: Tarmogoyf
Tarmogoyf.notes: Consistently a 4/5 or larger in a typical Vintage Cube game given the density of card types hitting graveyards. Exceptional rate for {1}{G}.

Tear Asunder.name: Tear Asunder
Tear Asunder.notes: Unconditional exile of any nonland permanent when kicked for {1}{B}{1}{G} is very strong removal. Even unkicked it answers artifacts and enchantments at instant speed. Flexible and powerful in BG shells.

Teferi, Hero of Dominaria.name: Teferi, Hero of Dominaria
Teferi, Hero of Dominaria.notes: One of the premier control finishers in the cube. The +1 draws a card and effectively untaps lands (enabling countermagic on the same turn), the -3 cleanly removes any nonland permanent, and the ultimate ends the game. A true signpost for WU and Esper control.

Teferi, Time Raveler.name: Teferi, Time Raveler
Teferi, Time Raveler.notes: Format-warping static ability shuts off opponent instants and flash, completely neutering reactive strategies. The +1 enables instant-speed wraths/sorceries, and the -3 provides efficient bounce with a free draw. Fits in any WU shell and actively hoses storm and control mirrors.

Temple Garden.name: Temple Garden
Temple Garden.notes: Fetchable dual land for GW. Core fixing for any deck running both colors. The 2-life payment is negligible. Rated B as a dual land — powerful but not a game-changer on its own.

Tendrils of Agony.name: Tendrils of Agony
Tendrils of Agony.notes: The primary storm kill condition in the cube. With a storm count of 9+ it deals lethal damage. Virtually unplayable outside storm, but an A-tier card within it as the main win condition.

Territorial Kavu.name: Territorial Kavu
Territorial Kavu.notes: Domain payoff; in the Vintage Cube's typical two-color environments it's often just a 2/2 or 3/3 for RG, but can scale up in multicolor shells. The attack trigger offers card filtering or graveyard hate, giving it modest value even at smaller sizes.

Tersa Lightshatter.name: Tersa Lightshatter
Tersa Lightshatter.notes: Haste + loot on ETB is solid aggro/tempo. The attack trigger replaying exiled cards at 7+ graveyard cards skews more toward self-mill/graveyard strategies, but the base stats and speed fit red aggro fine even without the graveyard clause.

Tezzeret, Cruel Captain.name: Tezzeret, Cruel Captain
Tezzeret, Cruel Captain.notes: Colorless Tezzeret self-grows from artifact ETBs (synergizes with fast mana), tutors Mox-level artifacts with −3, and the −7 can create a game-winning clock. Being colorless makes him a natural fit for any artifact-heavy deck regardless of colors.

Thassa's Oracle.name: Thassa's Oracle
Thassa's Oracle.notes: Instant-win combo with Paradigm Shift or Demonic Consultation/Tainted Pact (exile your whole library then Oracle wins on ETB). Even without the combo it's a serviceable 1/3 that scries. Its combo potential makes it format-defining.

The Endstone.name: The Endstone
The Endstone.notes: 7 mana is extremely expensive even in a powered cube. The draw-per-action effect is powerful but the life-total downside (halving to ~10) is severe. Only realistic in a fast-mana shell. Mostly too slow and risky.

The Mightstone and Weakstone.name: The Mightstone and Weakstone
The Mightstone and Weakstone.notes: Melds with Urza, Lord Protector for a powerful combined permanent, but both halves need to be in play simultaneously which is a high bar in cube. On its own, 5 mana for draw 2 or -5/-5 plus 2 colorless mana rocks is decent but not exciting.

The One Ring.name: The One Ring
The One Ring.notes: Indestructible + protection on entry buys a full turn of safety. The tap ability generates massive card advantage over time; the life loss is the balancing factor but decks that can close quickly or reset the ring (Welder) ignore it. Format-defining card draw engine.

The Wandering Emperor.name: The Wandering Emperor
The Wandering Emperor.notes: Flash planeswalker that can ambush attackers (exile tapped creature), create tokens, or pump blockers. Versatile across nearly every white archetype; the flash+instant-speed activations make her extremely difficult to play around.

Thespian's Stage.name: Thespian's Stage
Thespian's Stage.notes: The classic Dark Depths combo piece — copy Dark Depths with no ice counters and immediately sacrifice it for a 20/20 Marit Lage. Outside that combo it can copy any powerful land (Wasteland, Maze, etc.) for utility. Lands archetype signpost.

Thieving Skydiver.name: Thieving Skydiver
Thieving Skydiver.notes: Steals fast-mana artifacts or equipment for just 1UU+X. In a powered cube, often snatching a Sol Ring or Mox for very little investment is backbreaking. Flying body is also relevant for tempo.

Thopter Foundry.name: Thopter Foundry
Thopter Foundry.notes: Half of the classic Thopter-Sword engine with Sword of the Meek — sacrifice Sword, make a Thopter, Sword returns. Infinite life and infinite 1/1 flyers with enough mana. Even without the combo, a reliable source of chump blockers and life.

Thornspire Verge.name: Thornspire Verge
Thornspire Verge.notes: A functional RG dual that enters untapped conditionally (requires controlling a Mountain or Forest). Solid fixing for RG or Jund decks but not splashy enough to be more than a role-player. Worse than fetchlands or shocklands in most builds.

Thought Scour.name: Thought Scour
Thought Scour.notes: Primarily a cantrip that fuels the graveyard. Most valuable in reanimator (milling a fatty) or dredge, and in tempo decks where Snapcaster Mage turns the mill into a bonus.

Thoughtseize.name: Thoughtseize
Thoughtseize.notes: One of the best disruptive spells in the format. Fits nearly any black deck; the 2 life loss is largely irrelevant in control/midrange.

Thraben Inspector.name: Thraben Inspector
Thraben Inspector.notes: One of white's best one-drops; replaces itself via Clue, which also makes it surprisingly resilient in slower white shells.

Through the Breach.name: Through the Breach
Through the Breach.notes: Functional copies 5–8 of Sneak Attack in the cheat-a-fatty archetype. Instant speed is a big upside; the Splice mechanic is rarely relevant in Cube.

Thundering Falls.name: Thundering Falls
Thundering Falls.notes: A dual land for UR decks that enters tapped but replaces itself with a surveil 1, smoothing draws. The enters-tapped cost is a real downside in tempo builds.

Thundertrap Trainer.name: Thundertrap Trainer
Thundertrap Trainer.notes: Essentially a 2-mana Impulse on a body. The Offspring mode is expensive. Decent role-player in tempo/spell-heavy decks but not impactful enough for most cubes.

Tidehollow Sculler.name: Tidehollow Sculler
Tidehollow Sculler.notes: Being an artifact creature makes it synergize with Welder and artifact strategies. The leave-the-battlefield trigger is a known vulnerability but the tempo/disruption is strong.

Tifa Lockhart.name: Tifa Lockhart
Tifa Lockhart.notes: With landfall doubling power repeatedly in a turn (e.g., fetch + crack), she can deal huge trample damage. Best in ramp/landfall-heavy green decks.

Time Spiral.name: Time Spiral
Time Spiral.notes: Exiling itself means it doesn't loop, but untapping six lands and drawing 7 is an enormous storm enabler. The group wheel can occasionally help opponents, so context matters.

Time Walk.name: Time Walk
Time Walk.notes: Power Nine. An extra turn for {1}{U} is format-defining and fits into virtually every blue deck.

Time Warp.name: Time Warp
Time Warp.notes: Five mana is a steep cost compared to Time Walk, but an extra turn is always powerful. Loops with Eternal Witness in the right build.

Timetwister.name: Timetwister
Timetwister.notes: Power Nine. Wheeling both players for {2}{U} is a massive tempo and resource play. Best in storm (refuels after going off) and any blue deck that's ahead on board.

Tinker.name: Tinker
Tinker.notes: One of the most powerful cards in the Vintage Cube. Sacrificing a mana rock to fetch Blightsteel or Bolas's Citadel is frequently an instant win. The defining card of the tinker-combo archetype.

Tireless Tracker.name: Tireless Tracker
Tireless Tracker.notes: Generates massive card advantage in any deck that plays fetchlands or has land recursion. Grows into a serious threat over multiple turns. Best in green midrange shells that naturally trigger landfall repeatedly.

Tishana's Tidebinder.name: Tishana's Tidebinder
Tishana's Tidebinder.notes: Flash creature that can permanently silence a planeswalker's abilities or shut down an ETB trigger. Particularly powerful against Karn, Jace, or artifact mana abilities. The permanent-loss-of-abilities clause is very impactful in the right spots.

Titania, Protector of Argoth.name: Titania, Protector of Argoth
Titania, Protector of Argoth.notes: A powerhouse in the lands archetype. Recurring Wasteland or fetchlands while generating a stream of 5/3 tokens is extremely potent. The ETB that returns a land from the graveyard also has immediate value.

Tolarian Academy.name: Tolarian Academy
Tolarian Academy.notes: Banned in Legacy for a reason. In artifact-heavy decks this easily taps for 3-5+ blue mana, enabling explosive storm or Tinker turns. Actively warp-drafting signal for artifact and storm strategies.

Torsten, Founder of Benalia.name: Torsten, Founder of Benalia
Torsten, Founder of Benalia.notes: Very expensive at 7 mana. Requires both green and white. The ETB digs for creatures/lands and the death trigger makes seven 1/1 tokens, but at this cost you need serious ramp or a very dedicated payoff shell. Borderline cube-worthy.

Town Greeter.name: Town Greeter
Town Greeter.notes: Mills 4 and puts a land into hand, which has some marginal self-mill synergy for reanimator or dredge strategies. Weak body and conditional effect make this a fringe inclusion at best.

Toxic Deluge.name: Toxic Deluge
Toxic Deluge.notes: One of the best sweepers in the format. Unlike Wrath of God, it hits indestructible creatures and is a full mana cheaper, at the cost of life. Flexible X means it can be precision-scalpel or full board wipe. Staple in any black control or midrange deck.

Treasure Cruise.name: Treasure Cruise
Treasure Cruise.notes: Draw 3 for effectively {U} in any deck that fills the graveyard. Banned in Legacy and Modern. Pairs exceptionally well with spell-heavy UR Tempo shells that churn through cards quickly.

Trinket Mage.name: Trinket Mage
Trinket Mage.notes: Tutors for 0-1 mana artifacts, which in the Vintage Cube means Moxen, Sol Ring, Top, or Pithing Needle. A 2/2 body with a tutor ETB is consistently useful in blue artifact decks.

Troll of Khazad-dûm.name: Troll of Khazad-dûm
Troll of Khazad-dûm.notes: The swampcycling is the only reason this sees cube consideration — it can fix mana or thin the deck. The creature itself is overcosted and unimpressive for Vintage Cube power level.

Tropical Island.name: Tropical Island
Tropical Island.notes: Dual lands are generically format-staple mana fixers. No specific archetype tag since Tropical Island enables any Green-Blue or multicolor deck. Power tier A because untapped dual lands are among the most impactful cards in the cube for enabling consistent mana.

Tundra.name: Tundra
Tundra.notes: Dual lands are S-tier mana fixing. Tundra enables every WU and WUx archetype painlessly.

Twisted Landscape.name: Twisted Landscape
Twisted Landscape.notes: Enters tapped for colorless, tutors basics slowly, and cycles for 3 mana. Very low rate for Vintage Cube — only relevant in BRG decks that lack fixing and can tolerate the inefficiency.

Ugin, Eye of the Storms.name: Ugin, Eye of the Storms
Ugin, Eye of the Storms.notes: 7 mana is a steep cost but he exiles colored permanents on cast and every colorless spell you cast afterwards. Fits well in colorless ramp/artifact shells that can leverage the mana ability and -11 finisher.

Umezawa's Jitte.name: Umezawa's Jitte
Umezawa's Jitte.notes: One of the most powerful Equipment ever printed. Completely warps creature combat — generates charge counters quickly and answers opposing creatures with -1/-1, pumps your attacker, or gains life. A bomb in any creature-based deck.

Undercity Sewers.name: Undercity Sewers
Undercity Sewers.notes: Surveil 1 on ETB is a real upside in graveyard and control decks that want to filter their draws. Enters tapped, which is a meaningful cost in fast environments, but the UB fixing plus free surveil makes it solid in the right shells.

Underground Mortuary.name: Underground Mortuary
Underground Mortuary.notes: Similar to Undercity Sewers but in BG. The surveil pairs nicely with graveyard strategies. Enters tapped, so it's a cost in aggressive builds, but BG midrange and dredge-style decks appreciate the filtering.

Underground Sea.name: Underground Sea
Underground Sea.notes: Dual lands are S-tier mana fixing. Underground Sea is one of the most in-demand duals enabling every UB and UBx archetype.

Underworld Breach.name: Underworld Breach
Underworld Breach.notes: Effectively a second Yawgmoth's Will in storm/combo shells. The escape cost (pay the card's cost + exile 3) is very manageable when your graveyard is stocked. Combo-kills with Brain Freeze and Lion's Eye Diamond. A signpost for storm when seen in a pack.

Unearth.name: Unearth
Unearth.notes: Reanimates any creature with MV 3 or less — hits many of the best cube creatures. Cycling for 2 provides a safety valve when there's no good target. Solid in aggressive black decks and graveyard synergy shells alike.

Unexpectedly Absent.name: Unexpectedly Absent
Unexpectedly Absent.notes: Extremely flexible instant-speed answer to any nonland permanent. X=0 puts it on top of library (tempo play), higher X buries it deeper. Synergizes well with your own shuffle and library-manipulation effects to turn the 'just beneath top X' clause into a true exile.

Unholy Heat.name: Unholy Heat
Unholy Heat.notes: Delirium is very easy to achieve in Vintage Cube with fetchlands, artifacts, and looting effects. At 6 damage for 1 mana it answers nearly every creature and most planeswalkers. Without delirium it's a below-rate Lightning Bolt, but delirium is often online by turn 2-3.

Upheaval.name: Upheaval
Upheaval.notes: The classic 'Upheaval + float mana + Tog/artifact' lock. Bounces everything to reset the board while you retain mana advantage from fast mana. A signpost for stax/prison since it's really only a game-winner in that shell; it's a liability elsewhere.

Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth.name: Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth
Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth.notes: Primarily fixes black mana for any multicolor black deck and combos exceptionally well with Cabal Coffers if both are in the cube. Also benefits opponents, so it's best when you have the strongest payoff for B-heavy mana.

Uro, Titan of Nature's Wrath.name: Uro, Titan of Nature's Wrath
Uro, Titan of Nature's Wrath.notes: Combines lifegain, card draw, and land acceleration in one resilient threat. The escape clause makes it nearly impossible to permanently answer, making it a format-defining haymaker in any UG shell.

Ursine Monstrosity.name: Ursine Monstrosity
Ursine Monstrosity.notes: The self-mill and card-type graveyard synergy aligns it most with dredge or graveyard-value shells. The random attack direction is a notable drawback in multiplayer but largely irrelevant in 1v1 cube.

Urza, Lord High Artificer.name: Urza, Lord High Artificer
Urza, Lord High Artificer.notes: Taps artifacts for blue mana making him a mana engine with just a few artifacts on board, and the {5} activation can hit game-winning spells off the top. One of the most powerful artifact payoffs in the cube.

Urza's Bauble.name: Urza's Bauble
Urza's Bauble.notes: A free artifact that replaces itself a turn later. Excellent for Urza/artifact synergy decks, storm spell-count, and Urza's Saga tutor target. The hand peek is largely irrelevant; the artifact type and free draw are the value.

Urza's Saga.name: Urza's Saga
Urza's Saga.notes: Produces mana, creates Construct tokens that scale with artifacts, and tutors for any 0- or 1-mana artifact. An exceptional role-player and signpost in any artifact-centric deck.

Vampire Hexmage.name: Vampire Hexmage
Vampire Hexmage.notes: First strike on a 2/1 for BB is fine, but the primary cube reason to draft it is the instant Dark Depths combo — sacrificing it removes all ten ice counters, creating Marit Lage immediately.

Vampiric Tutor.name: Vampiric Tutor
Vampiric Tutor.notes: Instant-speed library tutor at one mana is among the most powerful effects in any format. Pairs with any powerful singleton target and enables consistent combo assembly.

Vaultborn Tyrant.name: Vaultborn Tyrant
Vaultborn Tyrant.notes: A 7-mana threat that generates value when it enters alongside other big creatures and leaves behind a copy of itself on death. Best in ramp or reanimator shells that already field power-4-or-greater creatures.

Vengevine.name: Vengevine
Vengevine.notes: Hasty 4/3 that recurs for free when casting two creatures. In dredge/graveyard shells it can represent multiple free 4/3s per turn cycle; even outside of that it's a resilient threat.

Verdant Catacombs.name: Verdant Catacombs
Verdant Catacombs.notes: Fetchland that finds Swamps and Forests; generic fixing for any BG-adjacent deck. Not archetype-specific but a universal mana-base staple.

Vindicate.name: Vindicate
Vindicate.notes: Sorcery speed is the only downside on an unconditional permanent removal spell. Hits lands, planeswalkers, artifacts, and creatures — extremely flexible in any WB shell.

Vivi Ornitier.name: Vivi Ornitier
Vivi Ornitier.notes: The mana ability snowballs quickly — each noncreature spell pumps power, enabling even more mana for chaining spells. Functions almost like a storm engine in UR tempo or combo shells. The damage ping is a meaningful bonus in race situations.

Voice of Victory.name: Voice of Victory
Voice of Victory.notes: The 'opponents can't cast spells during your turn' clause is a powerful combat-protection effect — your tokens and creatures attack freely. The Mobilize 2 generates immediate board presence each attack. Best in white weenie/aggro and token strategies.

Volcanic Island.name: Volcanic Island
Volcanic Island.notes: Dual land — one of the most valuable lands in the cube. Enables every UR and three-color strategy involving blue/red without any cost. Essentially a first-pick-quality card.

Voldaren Epicure.name: Voldaren Epicure
Voldaren Epicure.notes: A 1-mana 1/1 that pings all opponents and creates a Blood token is very efficient. The Blood token provides loot-style discard support for reanimator and dredge while the body is serviceable in aggro. Solid role-player across several archetypes.

Walk-In Closet // Forgotten Cellar.name: Walk-In Closet // Forgotten Cellar
Walk-In Closet // Forgotten Cellar.notes: Walk-In Closet's play-lands-from-graveyard effect is a strong signpost for a lands strategy, synergizing heavily with Crucible of Worlds and Life from the Loam. The back half (Forgotten Cellar) is a strong value engine if you can unlock it, enabling spells-from-graveyard effects. However, the Room mechanic is slow.

Walking Ballista.name: Walking Ballista
Walking Ballista.notes: Format-defining utility artifact. Scales with mana investment, doubles as a removal spell and a win condition. Synergizes with +1/+1 counter strategies and is a natural Tinker target in some builds. Near-universally playable in any deck with colorless mana.

Wan Shi Tong, All-Knowing.name: Wan Shi Tong, All-Knowing
Wan Shi Tong, All-Knowing.notes: The ETB is a tempo removal effect (not exile, but disruption). The token-generation trigger is slow and situational in most cubes. Best in controlling shells that can leverage the board presence over time. The 5-mana slot is contested.

Wan Shi Tong, Librarian.name: Wan Shi Tong, Librarian
Wan Shi Tong, Librarian.notes: Flash + flying + vigilance is a strong keyword combination. Enters as a flexible-sized threat and draws cards on opponent's fetchland/tutor activations. The draw-on-library-search trigger is very relevant in Vintage Cube given how often opponents fetch. Solid midrange tempo threat.

Wasteland.name: Wasteland
Wasteland.notes: One of the most powerful hate lands in the format. Slows greedy mana bases significantly and combos with Crucible/Loam for repeatable land destruction. First-pickable in aggressive and land-recursion strategies alike.

Wastewood Verge.name: Wastewood Verge
Wastewood Verge.notes: Conditional dual (requires controlling a Swamp or Forest) — weaker than a true dual or shockland but still serviceable in BG/Jund/Sultai. The condition is almost always met in its target archetypes, but it doesn't compare favorably to fetchable duals.

Waterlogged Grove.name: Waterlogged Grove
Waterlogged Grove.notes: Horizon land for GU — pays 1 life for colored mana and can sacrifice to draw in the late game. Excellent for avoiding flood in midrange and tempo decks. Slightly weaker than Fiery Islet or Sunbaked Canyon due to color combination, but still strong.

Watery Grave.name: Watery Grave
Watery Grave.notes: Shockland that's also an Island and Swamp — fetchable and enters untapped at the cost of 2 life. Staple dual land for any UB or three-color strategy. First-pickable as a fixing piece.

Wheel of Fortune.name: Wheel of Fortune
Wheel of Fortune.notes: One of the best draw spells in the cube. Refills your hand for 3 mana but symmetric — best in decks that empty their hand quickly (red aggro, storm) or can abuse the discard (dredge, reanimator-adjacent). Also enables graveyard setups when opponents discard fatties.

Wight of the Reliquary.name: Wight of the Reliquary
Wight of the Reliquary.notes: Scales well in creature-heavy graveyard decks. The land-tutoring ability is a powerful mana sink that also enables land-combo synergies like Dark Depths. Best in BG midrange or Sultai where creatures fill the bin naturally.

Winds of Abandon.name: Winds of Abandon
Winds of Abandon.notes: Single-target mode is an efficient spot removal at 2 mana. Overloaded it becomes a one-sided wrath but gives opponents basic lands — pairing with Armageddon or land-denial effects is particularly punishing. The ramp given to opponents limits its ceiling.

Windswept Heath.name: Windswept Heath
Windswept Heath.notes: Universal mana fixing and deck thinning. Slots into any WG or multicolor deck. Synergizes with Crucible of Worlds and Wrenn and Six for repeated fetchland value.

Wishclaw Talisman.name: Wishclaw Talisman
Wishclaw Talisman.notes: Effectively a repeatable tutor in combo decks that can win the turn they find their missing piece. The 'opponent gains control' drawback is largely irrelevant if you win immediately; in storm/combo it often tutors on the winning turn. Weaker in fair decks since giving opponents a tutor is dangerous.

Witch Enchanter // Witch-Blessed Meadow.name: Witch Enchanter // Witch-Blessed Meadow
Witch Enchanter // Witch-Blessed Meadow.notes: The front face is a slow, expensive Disenchant stapled to a body. The backside MDFC land offers a painful but flexible white source. Useful in slower white decks that need artifact/enchantment hate, but the 4-mana front face is quite clunky for the cube's power level.

Wooded Foothills.name: Wooded Foothills
Wooded Foothills.notes: Universal mana fixing and deck thinning for any RG or multicolor deck. Synergizes with Crucible of Worlds and Wrenn and Six for repeated value.

Woodfall Primus.name: Woodfall Primus
Woodfall Primus.notes: Persist + noncreature permanent destruction makes this a nightmare to deal with once it's in play. Especially brutal with Melira for infinite loops, and a great reanimation/cheat target. The 8 mana hard-cast cost is prohibitive but rarely relevant in these strategies.

Worldspine Wurm.name: Worldspine Wurm
Worldspine Wurm.notes: One of the most threatening cheat targets in the cube — 15 power with trample that leaves behind 15 more power when it dies. The 'shuffle into library' clause makes it reanimator-unfriendly unless you resolve it from the cheat (Sneak Attack kills it at end of turn, triggering the tokens but sending it back to library). Best in Sneak Attack decks.

Wrath of God.name: Wrath of God
Wrath of God.notes: Format-defining sweeper. 'Can't be regenerated' is occasionally relevant. An automatic inclusion in any white control or midrange deck. Note: mono-white aggro sometimes runs it to reset after going wide with tokens or to punish opposing creature strategies.

Wrenn and Six.name: Wrenn and Six
Wrenn and Six.notes: Extremely efficient 2-mana planeswalker. The +1 recurring fetchlands or Wasteland is incredibly powerful. The -1 pings small creatures and planeswalkers. The ultimate generates a retrace emblem that is backbreaking. Signals lands archetype heavily when seen in pack but also a staple in any RG shell.

Xander's Lounge.name: Xander's Lounge
Xander's Lounge.notes: Tri-land for UBR with a cycling safety valve. Enters tapped which is a significant cost, but the cycling mitigates flood. Best in slower Grixis control builds that can afford the tempo loss. The cycling cost of 3 is expensive but occasionally relevant late game.

Yavimaya, Cradle of Growth.name: Yavimaya, Cradle of Growth
Yavimaya, Cradle of Growth.notes: Grants all lands Forest typing, enabling massive mana acceleration with Gaea's Cradle and fixing for green-heavy multicolor decks. Pairs devastatingly with Urborg (every land is both a Swamp and a Forest). Basically a free inclusion in any green deck.

Yawgmoth's Will.name: Yawgmoth's Will
Yawgmoth's Will.notes: One of the most powerful spells in the Vintage Cube. In storm it's effectively a second hand of every spell already spent that turn. The exile-instead clause requires careful timing but rarely matters. Essentially a combo-enabler for any black deck that can refuel with it.

Zagoth Triome.name: Zagoth Triome
Zagoth Triome.notes: Enters tapped, so it's a tempo loss in fast decks. Best in Sultai and other BUG/three-color midrange shells that can afford the slowness and benefit from the fetchable cycling late. Cycling for {3} is expensive but provides a minor safety valve.

Ziatora's Proving Ground.name: Ziatora's Proving Ground
Ziatora's Proving Ground.notes: Jund's triome. Enters tapped which is a real cost in aggressive builds, but smoothing BRG mana for Jund midrange is extremely valuable. Fetchable by any black, red, or green fetchland.

Zirda, the Dawnwaker.name: Zirda, the Dawnwaker
Zirda, the Dawnwaker.notes: The companion requirement (every permanent must have an activated ability) is extremely restrictive in cube and rarely satisfiable. Its main use case is as a combo piece with Basalt Monolith (reducing its untap to zero mana = infinite colorless mana). Outside that specific combo it's too narrow to reliably include.

Zuran Orb.name: Zuran Orb
Zuran Orb.notes: Zero-mana artifact that sacrifices lands for life. Mostly a combo piece with Fastbond (gain 2 life to offset the 1 damage, enabling unlimited land drops) or Titania (sacrifice lands for 5/3 tokens). In pure life-gain decks it's marginal, but in the Fastbond/Titania/Crucible engine it can be format-warping.
